segments.integrated_caption: The video begins with a close-up of a Meraki dashboard interface, displaying network monitoring data, as a male presenter in a dark blazer and glasses gestures in a small inset window, stating, "the Meraki dashboard." He receives an audible "woo" from the audience, acknowledging the ability to "analyze" and "fix things so much faster" with the network. The scene transitions to a full-screen slide featuring L'Oréal with the tagline 'The number one beauty company in the world,' accompanied by a close-up of a woman’s face and a hand applying product. The presenter announces, "And one company on that journey is L'Oréal," noting their long-standing relationship as a Cisco customer and their embrace of the "platform approach and the Cisco networking cloud," calling it "beautiful to see a 100-year-old company embracing technology to transform" before instructing to "roll the video." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue stage lighting, where the same presenter stands on a circular stage, speaking to a seated audience, flanked by large screens displaying the L'Oréal slide. The presenter, now in a full-body shot, walks across the stage with expressive hand gestures, illuminated by vertical neon light strips. A new male voice begins speaking, identifying L'Oréal as "the largest beauty company in the world today" and emphasizing IT as an enabler to become "the number one beauty tech company." The video cuts to a close-up of a man applying a product to a woman’s hair, with the word 'SCAN' overlaid, followed by a montage of L'Oréal’s digital and manufacturing operations, including a mobile app interface for hair analysis and automated production lines. The voice continues, detailing L'Oréal's "research departments, factories, retail, headquarters," and his responsibility for "providing the connectivity and the networks in all of those locations." The scene shifts to an interview with Charles Croft, identified as Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, who speaks directly to the camera in a modern office setting with red and white chairs in the background, continuing to explain his role. The video concludes with a final shot of Charles Croft speaking, with the Cisco Live! logo visible in the top right corner throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Charles Croft, Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office, explaining how past network complexities have evolved into high-availability solutions thanks to products like Cisco Meraki. He describes this as a comprehensive connectivity platform, extending beyond just network and Wi-Fi to include sensors and CCTV. As he speaks, the scene transitions to a retail environment where a woman seamlessly interacts with a digital display and a tablet at checkout, illustrating the customer experience enabled by such a platform. Charles continues, emphasizing the power of the dashboard and app, which allows him to remotely control any device in Europe from his mobile phone, a capability visually demonstrated by a hand using a smartphone to display real-time power metrics. He further illustrates this by mentioning managing devices from a London double-decker bus, which is shown traversing the city streets. The video then highlights the underlying technology with close-ups of Cisco networking hardware, including switches and a wall-mounted access point, before returning to Charles. He elaborates on how this technology simplifies operations, turning what used to be months-long pop-up setups into plug-and-play processes, underscoring the critical role of automation and APIs. A person is then shown working at a desk with dual monitors displaying network management software, visually representing these advanced capabilities. The segment culminates with a graphic proudly stating '3500 devices in 3 days,' perfectly aligning with Charles's enthusiastic remark about patching that many devices in such a short timeframe, before he delivers his final remarks. Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Charles Croft, Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting. Dressed in a dark blazer over a floral shirt, he explains how "doing things quicker makes it much simpler and cheaper to do," noting that "the TCO of our legacy systems versus our Cisco Meraki systems, we reckon is about half." He emphasizes that "it costs us half to run exactly the same estate as it used to," concluding, "we do more with less." As he finishes, the scene transitions to a wide-angle view of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where a circular stage is illuminated by blue lighting and surrounded by a massive audience. Vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green enhance the dynamic, high-tech atmosphere. Applause and cheering erupt from the audience, many wearing lanyards, with some recording the event on their smartphones. Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, walks onto the stage, dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers. He gestures expressively as he addresses the crowd, with large screens behind him displaying his image and the Cisco Live! branding. He acknowledges the applause, stating, "That's wonderful." He then highlights that "L'Oréal is not the only company that sees new possibilities with this dramatic simplification," mentioning that "both OpenAI and L'Oréal are rolling Meraki out across their entire enterprise infrastructure." He continues, "Now we are redefining cloud-based secure connectivity for the branch and the campus by integrating the campus, the Catalyst SD-WAN, and the Cisco..." as a bold announcement slide appears on the curved video screen above the stage, prominently displaying 'ANNOUNCING Secure Access with Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN,' signaling a major product reveal.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Jonathan Davidson, a man in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt, stands center stage, illuminated by blue lights, addressing a massive audience. He speaks clearly, introducing "Cisco Secure Access, our secure services edge solution," explaining it works for "branch internet" and "SAS traffic." As he details how to "connect branches to the Cisco Secure Access across the SD-WAN fabric," the large curved video screen above him transitions to a software interface. This interface first displays the 'Cisco Secure Access Credential' dialog box for API key and secret input, followed by a 'Save' action. The screen then shows the 'Policies' page, highlighting the 'US-West Branches' policy group with 'Embedded Security' set to 'Cisco_Secure_Access_Connectivity'. Jonathan clicks 'Deploy', and a 'Success' notification confirms the policy was "deployed to 20 sites," which he states is "literally that easy" and enables "rapidly deploy scalable, highly secure, sassy-based architectures with just a few clicks." A vibrant graphic with 'Let’s go' and the Cisco Live! logo briefly appears before returning to Jonathan on stage. He continues his presentation, now with a multi-panel security dashboard displaying live surveillance footage on the large screen. He explains that "the role of IT is expanding beyond cybersecurity," and how "customers count on Wi-Fi enabled, AI capable Meraki cameras for physical security," citing a "European rail operator" who uses them to "secure their own operations." Throughout, Jonathan moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated gestures, while vertical neon strips in red, blue, and green enhance the high-tech atmosphere, and the screens display his image from multiple angles.

The large curved video screen above the stage displays his image and transitions to a software interface for Cisco Secure Access, showing policy configurations and credential input. The interface highlights the 'Cisco Secure Access Credential' dialog box, where an API key and secret are entered, followed by a 'Save' action. The screen then shows the 'Policies' page with a 'US-West Branches' policy group, where the 'Embedded Security' is set to 'Cisco_Secure_Access_Connectivity'. The speaker clicks 'Deploy', and a 'Success' notification appears, confirming the policy was deployed to 20 sites. The scene transitions to a vibrant, colorful graphic with the text 'Let’s go' and the Cisco Live! logo, before returning to the stage where the speaker continues his presentation, now with a large screen displaying a multi-panel security dashboard with live surveillance footage. The stage lighting includes vertical neon strips in red, blue, and green, enhancing the high-tech atmosphere. The speaker moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated gestures, while the large screens display his image from multiple angles, emphasizing the immersive presentation environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a detailed view of the Cisco Meraki Vision dashboard, a security monitoring interface displaying a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station on Friday, December 15th. Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, begins speaking, explaining in a clear, confident voice how thousands of cameras running 24/7 generate millions of hours of footage every week, posing the challenge of making it meaningful. The interface shows filters for time, cameras, people (with color-coded clothing options), and vehicles, as Davidson emphasizes that sorting through footage to isolate one person or event could take days, but AI is able to solve this problem within seconds. The scene transitions to Davidson live on stage at Cisco Live!, standing on a dark stage illuminated by vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green. He gestures expressively to a large audience, his image projected on a large curved screen above. As he continues, the presentation returns to the Meraki Vision interface, where filters are adjusted to focus on people, including a slider for 'Object 1' from 'Rough match' to 'Exact match' and a 'Minimum event duration' slider. Davidson explains how the Meraki Vision portal can filter on objects like vehicles or people, and even go deeper with color attributes, giving the example of finding "the person in the red shirt." The video then cuts to a wide-angle surveillance view of a railway tunnel entrance with green safety mats on the tracks, as Davidson reveals that the same customer uses Meraki cameras to enable rail operations and safeguard restricted areas. He elaborates that the AI has been trained to recognize employee uniforms, so if someone enters a prohibited, designated area without the uniform, the AI will alert security. The final segment shows a split-screen view of the Meraki Vision interface in 'List View' mode, highlighting a specific event where a person in an orange jacket is seen on the train tracks, demonstrating the system’s ability to detect and flag potential safety incidents, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video begins with a detailed view of a security monitoring interface, specifically the Cisco Meraki Vision dashboard, displaying a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station on Friday, December 15th. The interface includes filters for time, cameras, people (with color-coded top and bottom clothing options), and vehicles (with type and color filters), allowing for refined event searches. The thumbnails show various scenes: people waiting on platforms, trains arriving, individuals walking through turnstiles, and a person near the tracks. The scene then transitions to a live presentation at Cisco Live!, where Jonathan Davidson, identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands on a dark stage with vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green. He gestures expressively while speaking to a large audience, with his image projected on a large curved screen above the stage. The presentation returns to the Meraki Vision interface, where filters are adjusted to focus on people, including a slider for 'Object 1' from 'Rough match' to 'Exact match' and a 'Minimum event duration' slider. The video then cuts to a wide-angle surveillance view of a railway tunnel entrance with green safety mats laid on the tracks, followed by a return to the stage where the speaker continues his presentation. The final segment shows a split-screen view of the Meraki Vision interface in 'List View' mode, highlighting a specific event where a person in an orange jacket is seen on the train tracks, demonstrating the system’s ability to detect and flag potential safety incidents. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment, reinforcing the event’s branding.

It is demonstrated through Jonathan Davidson's explanation of its ability to filter on objects, use color attributes (e.g., 'find me the person in the red shirt'), and detect safety incidents, such as a person in an orange jacket on train tracks, by recognizing unauthorized entry into prohibited areas without employee uniforms. The interface also features sliders for 'Object 1' match precision and 'Minimum event duration', and is shown in both grid and 'List View' modes.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic montage of a security monitoring dashboard, highlighting a 'Trespass incident on Platform 4' with real-time data, as Jonathan Davidson's voice begins, explaining how the system responds to 'that intrusion' without compromising privacy or using facial recognition. He details how 'automatic video encryption and authenticated firmware' make 'Meraki cameras more secure against threats,' emphasizing they are 'more than simply cameras,' but 'sensors that run machine learning models at the edge for applications beyond physical security.' The visual transitions to a vibrant 'Let’s go' graphic with the 'Cisco Live!' logo, then to Davidson on a circular stage in a darkened auditorium. Dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, he speaks passionately, using expressive hand gestures, as vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green illuminate the stage. He continues, stating, 'This is the power of AI computer vision integrated with the network, powered by the network, and improved by the network.' The camera alternates between close-ups of his animated delivery and wide shots revealing the expansive audience and massive curved video screens above the stage, which display his image. Davidson then lightens the mood, remarking, 'I think there was a camera lover in the back over there.' He poses a question to the audience: 'with all the demand for AI-powered applications, how do you simplify, secure, and scale your data centers and protect the network resiliency?' The screens above him then transition to a presentation slide titled 'Road to AI: Ethernet,' as he declares, 'Ethernet. An open Ethernet infrastructure delivers fast data transfers between...' setting the stage for the next topic.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a dynamic montage of a security monitoring dashboard, displaying multiple surveillance feeds from a transit station, including a highlighted 'Trespass incident on Platform 4' with real-time data and analytics. This transitions into a vibrant, colorful animated graphic with the text 'Let’s go' and the 'Cisco Live!' logo, signaling the start of a major presentation. The scene then shifts to a large, darkened auditorium where Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands on a circular stage, dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers. He speaks passionately, using expressive hand gestures to emphasize his points, as the stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green. The camera alternates between close-ups of his animated delivery and wide shots revealing the expansive audience and the massive curved video screens above the stage, which display his image and the presentation title 'Road to AI: Ethernet'. The atmosphere is high-tech and immersive, with dynamic lighting and a focus on innovation and connectivity.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic, fast-moving blurred background of city lights, overlaid with the bold white text 'Cisco leads the industry in open Ethernet,' establishing a high-tech, forward-moving theme. Jonathan Davidson, identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, immediately begins speaking, detailing how large-scale Ethernet-based fabrics improve interoperability, job completion time, energy consumption, and customer choice for AI workloads. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with a circular stage, where Jonathan, a man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, stands center stage. He gestures expressively as he speaks, his image projected on multiple large screens above and behind him, emphasizing Cisco's leadership in open Ethernet with products like Silicon One, Nexus, and the Cisco 8000. He states they've proven this technology for hyperscalers and enterprise AI, citing a Bloomberg survey that reveals 66% of enterprises using Ethernet for AI infrastructure are Cisco customers, significantly outperforming the next competitor at 3%. The audience, seated in rows, is visible in the foreground, many holding up phones to record, while the stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in blue, red, and green, creating a modern, immersive atmosphere. As Jonathan walks across the stage, the camera follows him from behind, capturing his confident stride and hand gestures. The presentation then cuts to a full-screen slide with a dark blue background and abstract blue and yellow shapes, featuring a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and the text 'Cisco and NVIDIA partner for secure AI infrastructure,' as Jonathan announces this major partnership with NVIDIA, Nutanix, and others to extend Cisco's leadership. The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently in the top right corner throughout, reinforcing the event branding.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a dark blue announcement slide featuring a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and white text proclaiming 'Cisco and NVIDIA partner for secure AI infrastructure,' set against abstract blue and yellow shapes. This transitions to a large screen displaying a quote from Jensen Huang, Founder and CEO of NVIDIA, stating: 'Working closely with Cisco, we’re making it easier than ever for enterprises to obtain the infrastructure they need to benefit from AI.' The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with a circular stage, where Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands center stage. Dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, Davidson speaks with expressive hand gestures and a clear, confident voice, explaining, "We all know that Nvidia is synonymous with AI and Cisco is the same with networking. And we're coming together on enterprise Ethernet networking solutions." As he moves dynamically across the stage, illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green, large curved screens display his image and the NVIDIA quote. Davidson continues, "We are putting Nvidia GPUs into Cisco servers and networking solutions so that you can design reliable, fast, scalable, sustainable architectures for AI workloads inside of your enterprise." The screens then transition to a new slide showing 'CISCO + NUTANIX' with the tagline 'Hyperconverged solution for hybrid cloud,' against a vibrant gradient background. Davidson emphasizes, "And then of course, we have our great partnership with Nutanix. Nutanix and Cisco have created a fully integrated and validated hyperconverged solution that includes compute, networking, and storage across data centers and clouds." The audience, visible in the foreground, watches attentively as he adds, "Nutanix also offers Nvidia GPU virtualization. All of this simplifies your infrastructure and enables faster application delivery. And we will strengthen this relationship even more by adding Nutanix licensing..." The high-tech and immersive atmosphere, with dynamic lighting and large-scale visuals, reinforces the theme of innovation and partnership throughout his presentation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, standing on a dark stage with vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green behind him. Speaking into a headset microphone with expressive hand gestures, he explains Cisco's EA buying programs and how Nvidia and Nutanix solutions are wrapped in Cisco Validated Designs. He elaborates that these designs are "end-to-end reference architectures" that take the "guesswork out of designing, deploying, and automating data centers to run efficient AI workloads," helping users "save both time and money" and supporting platforms like Red Hat, VMware, or Nutanix. As he speaks, the camera pulls back to reveal a large, circular stage in a dimly lit auditorium filled with an engaged audience. Multiple large screens display his image and the text 'Efficient AI workloads starts with Cisco Validated Designs' alongside a glowing blue Cisco logo, visually reinforcing his message. Davidson then transitions to discuss "scale," highlighting how a large hyperscaler in the US uses "Cisco's Ethernet network fabric for their AI workloads and their back-end networks," providing "power efficiency," "cost advantage," and "programmability." A close-up of a Cisco Silicon One™ chip on a circuit board, illuminated with blue light, emphasizes the hardware foundation of these advanced AI solutions. Throughout this high-tech and immersive presentation, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner, branding the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the Cisco Silicon One™ chip, a white square component labeled with the Cisco logo, glowing blue on a black circuit board, as Jonathan Davidson's voice declares it's "at the heart of this network AI fabric." The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a large, dark auditorium where Davidson, identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands on a circular stage, gesturing expressively. He explains how a "hyperscaler deployed a training cluster with the configuration that you see here," referring to the large curved screens behind him that display a 3D diagram of a network architecture, detailing '16 Cisco 8808 spine nodes, 252 leaf nodes and 4,032 GPUs' in a spine-leaf topology. Davidson emphasizes that this is "just the beginning," and Cisco's solutions "can work for any enterprise at any scale." A bold text overlay then proclaims 'Cisco is changing the very DNA of the internet' against a blurred city street, reinforcing Davidson's subsequent statement that "Cisco leads the way with the internet for the future." The camera returns to Davidson, who walks and gestures dynamically under dramatic blue and purple stage lighting, flanked by vertical neon light strips. He passionately describes how "the internet for the future is the backbone of digital experiences," where Cisco "stretches the boundaries of technology, with secure networks that scale to Zettabyte capacities and perform at terabits per second," and how customers rely on these networks for "five nines availability." The audience, seated in darkness, is visible in the foreground, engaged in the high-tech, immersive atmosphere, with the screens continuing to display the speaker and key messages, including the 'Cisco Live!' branding.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, speaking passionately on stage at Cisco Live! He wears a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt and gray trousers, gesturing expressively with his hands into a headset microphone against a black background. Davidson emphasizes that Cisco's innovations are "changing the very DNA of the internet," leading to "high performance, low cost, and significantly less energy usage." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide-angle view of the stage, showcasing a large, circular white platform surrounded by a dark auditorium filled with seated, engaged attendees. Multiple large curved screens above the stage display his image and presentation slides, which feature text like 'Cisco is changing the very DNA of the internet' and highlight 'colt — One of the largest service providers in EMEA,' alongside an image of a turquoise taxi with 'Connecting your business to the world.' Davidson specifically mentions Colt, stating they are "the largest providers in all of Europe" and that Cisco is helping them "lead in performance and in sustainability." He explains how Colt deployed "Silicon One and routed optical networking," achieving "five nines of service availability with automation, sub-50 millisecond recovery time," and "up to a 97% power savings," which he calls "humongous." The presentation transitions to a slide highlighting 'colt 97% power savings' with a scenic image of a green field with yellow flowers, symbolizing environmental impact. Davidson further adds that Colt will soon be calculating the power consumption and carbon footprint of each of their services based on Cisco's "Acdian solution." The stage is illuminated with blue and purple lighting, creating a high-tech atmosphere, and the camera alternates between close-ups of Jonathan Davidson speaking and wide shots of the stage, capturing the audience's engagement and the immersive visual setup. The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands center stage on a circular white platform. Dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, wearing glasses and an earpiece microphone, he speaks with expressive hand gestures. Behind him, a massive curved video screen initially displays his live image and a presentation slide featuring 'colt 97% power savings' over a scenic green field. Jonathan articulates the importance of "service visibility and insights with microsecond granularity" for faster launch and monetization of SLA-backed services, explaining how "automation, convergence, scalability, and infrastructure all come together to digitalize the entire world," accelerated by AI. The camera cuts to a close-up, highlighting his animated facial expressions and hand movements as he emphasizes his points. He then addresses the "barrier to that promise is complexity," which Cisco is solving with the "Cisco Networking Cloud." The scene transitions back to a wide view, with the screen now displaying the bold text 'If it’s connected, it’s protected.' followed by bullet points for 'Cisco Networking Cloud' and 'Cisco Security Cloud,' each with a cloud icon. Jonathan powerfully states that "Complexity is also the ally of bad actors," and Cisco is tackling this with the "Cisco Security Cloud." He vividly describes the common experience of fighting "bad actors," the "dread of a potential threat," "panic upon intrusion," and the "frenzied race to undo any potential damage," posing the question, "What if we could dampen down that cycle of dread, panic, and frenzy?" He concludes by noting that "Threats are growing across everything that connects," reinforcing the message of comprehensive network protection through Cisco’s cloud solutions to the attentive audience, all under dynamic blue stage lighting with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, speaking passionately on a dark stage illuminated by shifting vertical neon light strips. Wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, he gestures expressively, stating that if something "is connected, it must be protected," and that this unified approach drives the strategy for the Cisco Networking Cloud and Cisco Security Cloud, acting as a "force multiplier for simple, secure connectivity." As the camera pulls back, a large curved video screen above displays his image and title. He then invites "my friend Jeetu Patel, EVP and GM of Security and Collaboration," to the stage. Jonathan steps aside as Jeetu Patel, bald and wearing glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, walks onto the stage holding a small device. The audience, visible in silhouette, erupts in enthusiastic cheers and applause, accompanied by energetic music, as the curved screen updates to display Jeetu's name and title. Jeetu greets the audience, asking "How's everyone doing?" to more cheers, and reveals it's his "first time to Cisco Live!", describing the crowd as the "most fun, loud, boisterous audience" he'll ever encounter, engaging them with questions met by further enthusiastic claps and cheers. The Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner, maintaining a professional and energetic atmosphere.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, speaking passionately on stage at Cisco Live! He wears a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt and gray trousers, gesturing expressively with his hands as he addresses the audience. The stage is dark, illuminated by vertical neon light strips that shift colors—purple, blue, red, and green—creating a dynamic, high-tech atmosphere. As he moves across the stage, the camera pulls back to reveal a large, circular stage with a massive curved video screen above, displaying his image and title. The audience is visible in silhouette, seated in rows, attentively watching. The scene transitions as Jonathan steps aside, and Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager of Security and Collaboration, walks onto the stage. He is bald, wears glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small device in his hand. He gestures with his right hand as he begins his presentation. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, stands on a circular white stage. Dressed in a dark blazer over a black shirt, dark trousers, and black shoes, with clear-framed glasses and a bald head, he gestures expressively while holding a small device and speaking into an ear microphone. Vertical blue neon light strips illuminate the stage, and a massive curved video screen behind him displays a dynamic collage of colorful security company logos like 'CrowdStrike', 'Darktrace', 'Sophos', 'Axio', 'GoGuardian', and 'Rapid7'. The audience, seated in rows, watches attentively. Jeetu Patel articulately explains the challenge of managing "50 to 70 different products within their cybersecurity stack," calling it "frankly untenable" and stating it's "no longer something that's feasible to continue down that path." He describes a "major shift that's happening in the industry right now, of moving from a bunch of point solutions to integrated platforms." As he walks across the stage, engaging the audience with animated hand movements and occasionally adjusting his earpiece, he announces that "about 18 months ago or so, we announced the introduction of an integrated platform in security called the Cisco Security Cloud." The screen then transitions to a clean blue slide titled 'Cisco Security Cloud', featuring icons and text for 'User protection', 'Cloud protection', 'Breach protection', and 'Firewall'. He elaborates that this platform was "built to solve three key problems": "how do you protect the user within the organization?", "how do you protect cloud and cloud infrastructure?", and "how do you protect against a breach that might happen and, you know, very quickly detect the breach if it occurs, respond, remediate." The professional and high-tech atmosphere, with dynamic lighting and large-scale visuals, reinforces the theme of cybersecurity innovation, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, Cisco's EVP and General Manager of Security and Collaboration, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small device. He stands on a dimly lit stage with dynamic blue and purple vertical neon light strips, gesturing expressively as he speaks, adjusting his earpiece and moving across the stage. Jeetu discusses the importance of recovering from breaches as fast as possible, built on a core firewall foundation, and highlights the team's innovation in this area over the past 18 months, specifically focusing on what they've delivered to the market regarding "user protection." As he elaborates on the significant challenges in user protection, explaining how complicated it is for a "typical knowledge worker" to connect to various applications, the camera pulls back to reveal a wide-angle view of the large, circular stage surrounded by a massive, attentive audience in a darkened auditorium, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo visible. The large curved video screen behind him initially displays a presentation slide titled 'User protection' with a blue and yellow gradient background, then transitions to a diagram illustrating a mobile device interface with options labeled 'ZTNA', 'VPN', 'SaaS', and 'Direct', connected by colored lines to corresponding application categories: 'Private apps', 'Traditional apps', 'SaaS apps', and 'Internet apps', alongside an image of a woman using a tablet. Jeetu emphasizes that every single type of application requires a different connection method, making it complex for users. The camera alternates between wide shots of the high-tech stage and close-ups of Jeetu, who maintains an engaging and dynamic presence, using hand gestures to emphasize his points about application connectivity, while the audience remains attentive, creating an immersive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, stands on a large, circular stage at Cisco Live!, dressed in a dark blazer over a black shirt, dark trousers, and clear-framed glasses, holding a small device. The stage is dynamically lit with blue and purple neon strips, and a massive curved video screen behind him displays a presentation slide. This slide initially shows a mobile interface diagram with options like 'ZTNA', 'VPN', 'SaaS', and 'Direct' connecting to various app types, alongside a woman interacting with a tablet. As Jeetu Patel speaks with a clear, articulate, and professional voice, he gestures expressively, explaining how different communication modes like ZTNA and VPN create a "tremendous amount of cognitive load for the end user." He proposes a "better way" for organizations to function, suggesting a simpler process where users "authenticate to the system and you get to work." The presentation slide then transitions to highlight 'STEP 1: Authenticate' with a checkmark, visually reinforcing his point. He continues, describing a secure access application, integrated with ThousandEyes, that they built to "take care of the plumbing behind the scenes," ensuring users have the "same experience to go on and connect to any application." The camera alternates between close-ups of him and wide shots of the stage, capturing the engaged audience, while 'Cisco Live!' and a 'Live from the Keynote' banner remain visible, emphasizing the high-tech, professional atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, commands a circular white stage, dressed in a dark blazer and black shirt. He holds a small device and speaks with confident, expressive hand gestures, explaining that whether 'private or public, whether you're a contractor or an employee, whether you happen to be at home or at work, it's exactly the same experience.' Behind him, a massive curved video screen initially displays a 'STEP 1: Authenticate' slide, illustrating mobile device access to various app types, aligning with his discussion of handling 'the plumbing behind the scenes' after authentication. As he moves dynamically across the stage, adjusting his earpiece, the screen transitions to a vibrant, multicolored slide proclaiming 'Zero trust, zero friction.' Jeetu elaborates on this concept, defining 'Zero trust' as providing 'the least privileged access' and 'only the amount of rights that you need,' highlighting 'over-privileged access' as a 'huge challenge.' The stage is dramatically lit with blue and purple neon strips, and spotlights follow Jeetu, while the audience remains in darkness, focused on his presentation. He encourages attendees to explore 'the user protection side' and 'the demos... at the booth and the show floor,' reiterating the core message of 'zero trust with zero friction' before signaling a transition to the next topic. The Cisco Live! logo is consistently visible, reinforcing the high-tech event atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, stands on a dimly lit stage under dynamic blue and purple neon light strips at Cisco Live!, addressing a large audience. He holds a small device and speaks clearly, asking, "What do you do in cloud protection?" as the camera pulls back to reveal a vast auditorium with a circular stage and a massive curved video screen displaying 'Cloud protection' in bold white text against a blue and green gradient. Jeetu emphasizes the "same notion of zero trust" used for users connecting to applications, now extended to "applications talking to applications in the data center." As he explains that "a tremendous amount of applications have actually started moving to the cloud, whether it be an AWS or GCP or Azure," the screen transitions to a detailed diagram of a 'Private Cloud' environment, divided into 'Prod' and 'Dev' sections, listing components like 'Web Portal,' 'Finance,' 'PCI Data,' 'Sales Portal,' 'Sales,' 'Customer DB,' 'Web,' 'App,' and 'Database.' He highlights that "95% of the applications that happen to be in the cloud need to access some sort of resource in your private data center," illustrating this with an orange circle labeled 'AWS' containing a 'Marketing app' icon on the screen. Jeetu then poses, "Imagine if you happen to be a marketing application, you still need to go out and access a customer database that might be in your private data center." As he speaks, a red warning icon appears next to the 'Marketing app' on the screen, followed by a red dotted line connecting it to the 'Customer DB' in the 'Prod' section, visually demonstrating a security threat. The red lines then expand to show connections to multiple other components within the private cloud, illustrating the potential spread of the threat, as he describes how people "punch a hole through the firewall" to achieve this access. The stage lighting remains focused on Jeetu, with spotlights and the vibrant neon strips enhancing the high-tech atmosphere of the keynote presentation, with the audience attentively watching.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a detailed presentation slide displayed on a large curved screen, illustrating a 'Private Cloud' architecture divided into 'Prod' and 'Dev' environments, featuring components like 'Web Portal', 'Finance', 'PCI Data', 'Sales Portal', 'Customer DB', 'Web', 'App', and 'Database'. A red warning icon with an exclamation mark is prominently shown between the private cloud and an 'AWS' circle labeled 'Marketing app', indicating a security concern. Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, begins speaking, his voice clear and articulate, explaining how an application running on AWS might connect to one in a private data center. He highlights the 'downside': when it connects to the customer database, it gains access to every other application in the private data center, thus becoming 'no longer zero trust'. The slide evolves to show 'Speaks IPs' and 'Speaks Services' labels on either side of the warning icon, visually reinforcing his explanation that private data centers primarily use IP addresses while cloud applications are service-based, and these 'don't talk well to each other'. Jeetu, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, is then shown standing on a circular stage under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small device, gestures expressively, and occasionally adjusts his earpiece. The camera alternates between close-ups of him and wide shots of the engaged audience and the expansive stage setup, which features vertical neon light strips, spotlights, and large screens displaying the speaker and the evolving presentation. As he proposes a solution, asking 'what if we provided a translation layer for both of those?', the slide transitions again to show 'Cisco understands both' with a multi-colored icon replacing the warning. He continues, stating that this allows the marketing application to connect to the customer database via a 'trusted zero-trust connection', which is then visually represented by a 'Zero Trust' model with a green line labeled 'Authorized access only' connecting the 'Customer DB' to the 'Marketing app', emphasizing secure, controlled access within the high-tech, immersive atmosphere of the Cisco Live! event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small device. He stands on a dimly lit stage under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting, gesturing expressively as he begins, asking in a clear, articulate voice, "So breach protection, what do we do with breach protection?" He adjusts his earpiece and moves across the stage, explaining that "80% of breaches and attacks that happen in an organization start from email." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the stage, flanked by vertical neon light strips, showcasing a large, engaged audience seated in rows, some recording with smartphones. Jeetu vividly describes a phishing scenario: "There's some kind of funny looking email that you get from an exotic prince... that says click on this link, download your $10 million." He continues, detailing how clicking such a link "takes you to a website that didn't exist two hours ago... download some malware on your device and kickstarts a process... lateral movement within your organization." As he speaks, a large curved video screen above the stage transitions from his image to display a diagram with four connected boxes labeled 'Email', 'Web', 'Endpoint', and 'Network', each with corresponding icons, visually reinforcing the security architecture he discusses. Jeetu walks confidently, using hand gestures to emphasize that "That's how 80% of the breaches actually happen." He then addresses the industry challenge, stating, "The challenge is in this entire domain... there's 3500 vendors. There are vendors that do a really good job protecting email." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner, underscoring the professional, high-tech event environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, bathed in blue ambient lighting, creating a high-tech, immersive atmosphere. On a circular, illuminated stage, Jeetu Patel, dressed in a dark blazer over a black shirt, dark trousers, and polished black shoes, gestures expressively while holding a small device. He confidently moves across the stage, occasionally adjusting his earpiece. Behind him, a massive curved video screen initially displays a diagram with four connected boxes labeled 'Email', 'Web', 'Endpoint', and 'Network', each with corresponding icons and text like 'Every email', 'Every request', 'Every process', 'Every flow'. Jeetu Patel speaks clearly and confidently, explaining the challenge of isolated telemetry: "what you see in email isn't correlated with what's happening on the web." He then poses the solution: "can we correlate this telemetry to get better intelligence?" As the camera alternates between wide shots and close-ups, emphasizing his dynamic delivery, the screen transitions to a full view of this security architecture flow diagram. He elaborates on the benefit of having "knowledge of every email and every forward, of every request that happens on the web, of every process that gets initiated on your client, and of every packet that flows through the network." He concludes by illustrating how combining "a low-level alert that you might have ignored on an email with a low-level alert that you might have ignored on a website... actually translates to a high-level alert that you shouldn't be ignoring." Vertical neon light strips in blue and purple flank the stage, adding to the modern aesthetic, while the Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, stands on a large, circular stage at Cisco Live!, dressed in a dark blazer, black shirt, dark trousers, and clear-framed glasses, holding a small device. The stage is bathed in deep blue lighting with vertical neon strips, creating a high-tech ambiance. He speaks with a clear, articulate voice, explaining, "And so that's what we did when we actually launched XDR," praising his team for their "fantastic job" and noting the "tremendous amount of momentum" with customers. Behind him, a massive curved video screen displays a presentation slide titled 'XDR' with abstract blue and teal wave graphics. Jeetu gestures expressively, moving across the stage, encouraging the audience to "go see the demo again." He emphasizes the need to go "a step further" beyond detection and response, asking, "what do you need to do to make sure that you have some kind of a vaccination against ransomware?" The screen transitions to 'XDR + RECOVERY' in white and green text as Jeetu elaborates, "You want to make sure that if something happens and if you are ransomed, you can revert back to where you used to be." He announces, "And so we launched XDR plus recovery with partnership with some of our partners like Cohesity." The presentation continues with a diagram illustrating a suspicious process attempting to connect to an app, highlighting Cisco’s security architecture, which Jeetu begins to explain: "Let me show you how this works. This is actually really cool. All right, so this is how it works. Let's say that you have a suspicious process in um..." The stage setup includes multiple screens showing Jeetu’s image from different angles, reinforcing the immersive, professional atmosphere, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a large curved screen displaying a presentation slide titled 'Suspicious process attempts to connect to app,' illustrated with binary code, a warning symbol, and an app icon. Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, then appears on a dynamically lit stage, confidently speaking in a clear, authoritative voice. He explains that while 'known good or known bad' processes are easy to address, the real challenge lies in the 'gray area' of suspicious activity. As he gestures expressively, the slide evolves to show a 'Connection made, app infected' scenario, depicting a red bug in the app, contrasting with a secure version. Jeetu elaborates on how Cisco addresses this by immediately taking a 'snapshot' of the environment when a suspicious process is detected. He describes how, if the process turns out to be 'malware' infiltrating the environment, they can 'revert back' to a 'near instantaneous recovery' from the snapshot, achieving an 'RPO of zero' due to the inexpensiveness of snapshots. The camera alternates between the evolving slides and wide shots of Jeetu interacting with the engaged audience under vibrant blue and purple neon lighting. The presentation culminates with a new slide, 'The SOC of the future with SIEM + XDR + SOAR + UBA,' as Jeetu walks across the stage, emphasizing the integration of these security technologies in the high-tech, immersive setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, bathed in blue ambient lighting, featuring a circular stage and a massive curved video screen displaying 'The SOC of the future with Splunk' and 'SIEM + XDR + SOAR + UBA'. The attentive audience fills the foreground as the camera zooms in on Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco. Dressed in a dark blazer and black shirt, wearing clear-framed glasses, he walks confidently across the stage, holding a small device and gesturing expressively, occasionally adjusting his earpiece. Under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting, Jeetu explains the "beauty" of the system, detailing how it provides "a completely clear way" to recover from ransomware attacks and "have a vaccination" to restore the environment, all "happening through AI." He then addresses the role of XDR and Splunk, noting that "Splunk has the log data in their SIM system," while "we have XDR data." He further elaborates on Splunk's "sore data" for "orchestrating and automating an environment" and the inclusion of "user behavior analytics," concluding that "with Splunk, we actually pull all of these into a single platform." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible, reinforcing the event's branding as Jeetu's presentation visually and audibly emphasizes the integrated security solutions.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device. The camera pulls back to reveal a large, curved video screen above the stage displaying the title 'The SOC of the future with Splunk' and the components 'SIEM + XDR + SOAR + UBA' in bold white text. The circular white stage is surrounded by a large audience seated in darkness, with Jeetu’s image mirrored on multiple screens. He continues, noting that after "Jonathan went through what we're doing with AI infrastructure," Cisco has ensured they are "innovating in every layer of AI" and have made AI "pervasive throughout the entirety of the platform." The presentation transitions to a new slide titled 'Making AI pervasive across the Cisco Security Cloud,' featuring three interconnected buttons labeled 'Assist,' 'Augment,' and 'Automate,' each with an icon. The atmosphere is high-tech and immersive, with spotlights and a modern, minimalist design, while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event’s branding. Jeetu concludes by stating there are "three things we can do with AI in security," promising a demo soon.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a presentation slide titled 'Making AI pervasive across the Cisco Security Cloud,' featuring three interconnected buttons labeled 'Assist,' 'Augment,' and 'Automate,' each with corresponding icons. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with a circular stage, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, stands confidently holding a small black device. He gestures expressively as he speaks, his image projected on multiple large screens surrounding the stage. He explains, "person who's managing a security infrastructure has an an assistant. A sidekick, someone who can actually help them... in making sure that their job is easier. So the first thing that we do is assist. The second area is around augmenting data with with the human in the loop. And then the third one is fully automating certain tasks as well." The audience, seated in rows, watches attentively under blue ambient lighting. The presentation progresses as the slide changes to 'Cisco AI Assistant for Security,' displayed in bold white text against a vibrant gradient background of blue, orange, and yellow. Jeetu continues, "what we did was we have actually now launched a security-based assistant and we are calling it the Cisco AI Assistant for Security. It's not a multiple set of different assistants. It's one assistant that spans all of our security portfolio. For the security cloud." He engages the audience with dynamic hand movements, occasionally adjusting his earpiece, while the stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in blue and purple, creating a high-tech, immersive atmosphere. He then asks, "And so let me show you a demo of what this looks like. This is super cool. You ready?" The audience responds with an audible "Yeah!" Jeetu playfully remarks, "This is a very quiet crowd. Are you ready?" prompting a louder "Yeah!" from the attentive audience, before he concludes, "All right. This is a good crowd." The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, stands on a stage illuminated by vertical blue and purple neon light strips, holding a small black device. He enthusiastically declares, "This is a cool demo. I promise you're gonna like it." He then prompts the audience to "Imagine if you had an interface like this, which is just like Chat GPT but for your security environment." The scene transitions to a close-up of the 'AI Assistant' software interface, featuring a blue circular logo and the prompt 'What do you want to ask today?' Jeetu explains it's a "prompt interface" where users can "in natural language enter a prompt." As he speaks, a user types the query: 'I need to update our firewall policy. What policies are controlling the Sales APP?' Jeetu continues, stating, "immediately what you're gonna get back is a list of, here are the policies controlling access to that sales application." The AI Assistant responds with a list of access control policies like 'Application_Access' and 'Edge_Control_Application,' detailing their status and modification dates. Jeetu emphasizes its "completely multi-modal way" and describes it as a "beautiful kind of table" and a "rich media interface." He then poses a new scenario, and the interface shows a query: 'Add a rule to Application_Access that blocks outbound traffic from the SalesApp,' followed by a suggestion to block all outbound traffic with 'Yes' or 'No' options. Throughout, Jeetu gestures expressively, adjusting his earpiece and walking across the stage, while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner against dynamic, colorful abstract shapes.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where a speaker, identified as Jeetu Patel, stands on a stage illuminated by vertical blue and purple neon light strips. He is bald, wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device. The audience is seated in rows, many wearing lanyards, and the stage features a large screen displaying the speaker’s image. The scene transitions to a close-up of a software interface titled 'AI Assistant,' which displays a blue circular logo and prompts the user with 'What do you want to ask today?' with several suggested questions like 'Access Control Rule Summary' and 'Schedule Recurring Backups: A How-To Guide.' The speaker gestures expressively as he explains the interface, and the screen shows a user typing a query: 'I need to update our firewall policy. What policies are controlling the Sales APP?' The AI Assistant responds with a list of access control policies, including 'Application_Access' and 'Edge_Control_Application,' detailing their status and last modification dates. The speaker continues to explain, and the interface then shows a new query: 'Add a rule to Application_Access that blocks outbound traffic from SalesApp,' followed by a suggestion to block all outbound traffic from SalesApp with options to 'Yes' or 'No.' The speaker remains engaged, gesturing and speaking into a microphone, while the interface displays the AI Assistant’s response. The background features dynamic, colorful abstract shapes, and the 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner.

audio_updated_entities.AI Assistant Interface: A software interface titled 'AI Assistant' with a blue circular logo, displaying prompts like 'What do you want to ask today?' and providing responses to user queries about access control policies and firewall rules, including options to create new rules. It is described by Jeetu Patel as a 'prompt interface' that allows users to 'in natural language enter a prompt' and receive 'a list of' relevant information, functioning as a 'rich media interface' for security environments.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, presents on a stage illuminated by dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively, explaining how the AI Assistant automatically suggests rules, stating, "it automatically gives you a suggestion of what that rule would look like." On the main screen, the AI Assistant interface displays a suggestion to block outbound traffic from a 'SalesApp' for all users. Jeetu continues, "You hit yes, and voila, you've actually gone out and made sure that that rule of blocking outbound traffic from the SalesApp has been implemented right away." As he speaks, the interface shows a 'Yes' selection, followed by a confirmation message: 'Congratulations, Block-Outbound-SalesApp rule has been successfully created!' The assistant then asks if further help is needed. Jeetu then poses a new scenario, asking, "Now, what if you say, give me a step-by-step set of instructions on how to upgrade my firewall?" A new query is typed into the AI Assistant: 'Give me step-by-step instructions to upgrade the firewall.' The assistant responds with a detailed nine-step guide for upgrading the Cisco Secure Firewall, which Jeetu confirms, "What it'll do is it'll give you a full step-by-step set of instructions." He elaborates that "we've trained the machine learning model to say, what are the most common tasks that people do." The stage environment features vertical neon light strips and a marked stage area, with the Cisco Live! branding visible in the top right corner. The audience is partially visible, seated and engaged, with some recording the presentation on their devices.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment features Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, presenting on a stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively while speaking. On the main screen, an AI Assistant interface is displayed, showing a suggestion to block outbound traffic from a 'SalesApp' for all users, with options to create the rule. After a 'Yes' selection, a confirmation message appears: 'Congratulations, Block-Outbound-SalesApp rule has been successfully created!' The assistant then asks if further help is needed, and a new query is typed: 'Give me step-by-step instructions to upgrade the firewall.' The assistant responds with a detailed nine-step guide for upgrading the Cisco Secure Firewall, including logging into the Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC), navigating to device management, selecting the upgrade version, copying the upgrade package, monitoring progress, and running a readiness check. The stage environment includes vertical neon light strips and a marked stage area, with the Cisco Live! branding visible in the top right corner. The audience is partially visible, seated and engaged, with some recording the presentation on their devices.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a dark-themed AI Assistant interface, displaying step-by-step instructions for upgrading a firewall via the Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC), with a prompt to 'Ask the AI Assistant a question.' Simultaneously, Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, is seen on stage holding a small black device, gesturing expressively as he speaks, explaining how natural language interfaces make firewall administration easier, asking the audience, "How cool is that? Is that cool or what? It's amazing, this is great!" The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium, illuminated by blue stage lights, where Jeetu continues his presentation on a large central screen, emphasizing that "we're not done though." The camera returns to the AI Assistant interface, where a notification for 'Policy Analysis and Optimization' appears. Jeetu's voice explains that the AI Assistant is smart enough to identify 323 duplicate rules (153 shadowed, 170 redundant) out of 1234 total rules, asking "So what do you want to do with those rules? You want to delete the duplicate rules? Do you want to disable those duplicate rules?" The interface offers 'Disable all' and 'Delete all' buttons. After 'Delete all' is selected, a confirmation states 'All 323 duplicate rules have been disabled,' followed by a prompt to deploy changes to 4 devices. The user selects 'Yes,' and the interface confirms with 'Congrats, all changes have been deployed!' Jeetu concludes by highlighting how the system provides a "reasoning engine" for ongoing policy hygiene, allowing users to manage policies they "weren't able to do before."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment begins with a close-up of an AI Assistant interface, displaying step-by-step instructions for upgrading a firewall via the Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC). The interface is dark-themed with white text, and a prompt at the bottom invites users to 'Ask the AI Assistant a question.' In a smaller inset on the right, Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer over a black shirt, is seen on stage holding a small black device, gesturing as he speaks. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium filled with an audience, illuminated by blue stage lights. Jeetu Patel is now visible on a large central screen, continuing his presentation. The camera returns to a close-up of the AI Assistant interface, where a notification for 'Policy Analysis and Optimization' appears, revealing that 323 duplicate rules (153 shadowed, 170 redundant) were found out of 1234 total rules. The interface offers 'Disable all' and 'Delete all' buttons. After the 'Delete all' action is selected, a confirmation message states 'All 323 duplicate rules have been disabled,' followed by a prompt asking if the user wants to deploy the changes to 4 devices. The user selects 'Yes,' and the interface confirms with 'Congrats, all changes have been deployed!' The entire sequence demonstrates the AI Assistant's capability to automate firewall policy optimization and deployment, with Jeetu Patel guiding the audience through the process on stage.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a dimly lit stage under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He gestures expressively while speaking, holding a small black device, and occasionally adjusts his earpiece. His voice is clear and confident as he discusses new innovations. The large curved video screen behind him displays his image and then transitions to a slide titled 'ANNOUNCING New innovations in AI' with a green banner and bullet points: 'Securing AI: intellectual property protection,' 'Advanced AI email threat detection,' and 'AI Assistant in Cisco Secure Access.' Jeetu elaborates on these points, stating, "we have AI assistant for Cisco Secure Access. Just like you saw with firewall, that same assistant is now also going to be able to allow you to go out and set policies for your Sassy product and your SSC product through an AI assistant." He emphasizes that this AI assistant is "embedded across all of our products." The screen then shows a detailed interface of Cisco Secure Access, featuring an 'Access Policy' dashboard with a pop-up window for the 'Cisco Assistant' prompting users to create access rules. The scene shifts to a broader view of the stage, revealing the full scale of the auditorium with multiple screens and a circular stage, while the audience in the foreground remains engaged, some recording on smartphones. The next slide displays 'Cisco Security Cloud' with four protection layers: 'User protection,' 'Cloud protection,' 'Firewall,' and 'Breach protection,' each with corresponding icons. Jeetu continues, explaining, "basically what you have is a Cisco Security Cloud that's embedded with AI everywhere that you go. It's in the fabric of the Cisco Security Cloud." He then shifts to a "big problem that we wanted to make sure that we solved for, which was what typically is the biggest attack vector that you're seeing right now, where a lot of breaches are happening... The new attack vector that we're seeing is around identity." The atmosphere is professional and high-tech, underscored by the consistent 'Cisco Live!' branding in the top right corner of the screen, as the camera alternates between close-ups of Jeetu and wide shots of the stage and audience.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit, high-tech auditorium at Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, stands on a circular stage bathed in dynamic blue and purple neon lights. He holds a small black device and speaks with a clear, authoritative voice, declaring, "Identity, you know, a lot of people say identity is the new perimeter." He emphasizes this point as a large screen behind him displays 'Identity is the new attack surface.' He continues, stating, "In fact, 74% of the attacks that are happening involve a human element," a statistic visually reinforced by a close-up of intense eyes with the overlay '74% of breaches involved the human element, which includes social engineering attacks,' sourced from the Verizon 2023 Data Breach Investigations Report. Jeetu walks across the stage, adjusting his earpiece, as he explains, "There's some kind of social engineering that's actually part of that entire kind of process." He then poses a provocative question, which appears on screen: 'Why hack in... when you can simply log in?' He elaborates, "Typically what ends up happening is these adversaries, the threat actors, are saying, 'Hey, why do I need to hack in if I can just steal someone's credentials and simply log in?' It's a whole lot easier to do that." The next slide, titled 'Social engineering with voice cloning' and featuring a soundwave icon, appears as he describes a scenario: "Typically what you'd have is some kind of a social engineering attack where someone would say, 'I'm going to go out and impersonate a voice through some deep fake and voice cloning and call a help desk and make sure that when I've called the help desk, I've had them essentially reset my MFA password.'" He adds, "By the way, there is a few high-profile breaches that happened recently that happened in..." The professional atmosphere is maintained with the Cisco Live! logo visible, and the audience remains focused on the speaker and evolving visual content.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, stands on a circular stage. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively as he speaks. The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting, and large curved screens display his image and presentation slides. The first slide reads 'Identity is the new attack surface' in white text against a blue and orange gradient background. The scene transitions to a close-up of a man’s intense eyes, with a bold statistic overlay: '74% of breaches involved the human element, which includes social engineering attacks,' sourced from the Verizon 2023 Data Breach Investigations Report. The camera then cuts back to Jeetu Patel, who continues his presentation, walking across the stage and adjusting his earpiece. The next slide appears with the provocative question, 'Why hack in... when you can simply log in?' in white text on a dark blue background. This is followed by a slide titled 'Social engineering with voice cloning,' featuring a soundwave icon and a green accent line. The presentation continues with Jeetu Patel engaging the audience, using hand gestures to emphasize his points, while the large screens display the evolving slides. The atmosphere is professional and high-tech, with the audience seated in darkness, focused on the speaker and the visual content. The Cisco Live! logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event’s branding.

audio_updated_entities.Jeetu Patel: A bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device; he gestures expressively while speaking on stage with a clear, authoritative voice, adjusting his earpiece and walking across the stage under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device as he speaks on a large, dimly lit auditorium stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting and the 'Cisco Live!' logo visible. He articulates clearly, explaining an attack chain: "And then, what you'll see is you added a new device with that MFA password reset that happened, and then all of a sudden, access was granted... and then everyone, the person got unhindered access into your system. And now, they can do whatever they want to do in the system because they've actually gone in." As he speaks, the camera cuts to a presentation slide detailing this five-step attack chain: 'Social engineering with voice cloning,' 'MFA reset and add new device,' 'Access granted,' 'Unhindered system access,' and 'Ransomware, lost IP and more,' each step appearing sequentially with corresponding icons and color-coded progress bars. Jeetu continues, "The question that we've all been asking as we've gone through this entire, um, you know, kind of past three decades with identity is, can someone, does Jeetu Patel have the ability to log into this application? Let's go verify that typically at the point of time of authentication. Once you authenticate, you're in, you're free to do whatever you want." The scene transitions to a wide shot of Jeetu on a circular platform, his image projected on large curved screens above, with the audience seated in rows under spotlights and ambient blue lighting. He gestures expressively, adjusting his earpiece, as the presentation slide changes to text reading 'We’ve been asking can someone have access,' followed by 'We need to be asking should they have access...' He concludes, "And I think it's the wrong question to be asking. The question should be, should that person have access, and can we assess based on context and behavior?" The camera alternates between close-ups of Jeetu and wide shots, capturing the immersive, high-tech atmosphere of the Cisco Live! event.

audio_updated_entities.Jeetu Patel: A bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device; he speaks on stage with a clear, confident American accent, describing a five-step attack chain involving social engineering and MFA resets, leading to unhindered system access and ransomware. He then shifts to a philosophical discussion about identity, questioning whether the focus should be on 'can someone have access' versus 'should they have access,' even referring to himself in the third person ('does Jeetu Patel have the ability to log into this application?'). segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, speaking passionately and confidently on a dimly lit stage. He gestures expressively with a small black device, adjusts his earpiece, and articulates the need to assess if someone 'should be continually granted access' based on their behavior. As he speaks, a full-screen presentation slide appears, echoing his words: 'We’ve been asking can someone have access. We need to be asking should they have access...continuously,' adorned with abstract blue and yellow wave graphics. The scene transitions to a wide shot, revealing Jeetu on a circular platform before a large, curved video screen displaying his image and the slide, with an attentive audience in the foreground. He continues, stating the need to 'fundamentally reimagine identity-based trust,' which is then displayed on a new slide with a vibrant blue, orange, and yellow gradient. Jeetu then excitedly announces, 'I'm delighted to announce Cisco Identity Intelligence being launched today here in Amea, in Amsterdam,' as the screen updates to 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' with a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and descriptors: 'Open | Human & machine | Behavior-aware.' The audience responds with enthusiastic applause. Jeetu walks across the stage, gesturing towards the screen, and elaborates that Cisco Identity Intelligence will be 'completely open,' working with 'any identity provider,' covering 'not just human identity but also machine and service identity,' and will be 'behavior aware.' The camera alternates between wide shots of the dynamic stage, illuminated by blue and purple neon lights, and close-ups of Jeetu, maintaining the 'Cisco Live!' logo in the top right corner throughout, emphasizing the innovative and professional atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, stands on a dimly lit stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively as he speaks about intercepting "odd behavior." He explains, "here's how it works," as the camera cuts to a presentation slide displaying a colorful grid of technology company logos including Microsoft, Cisco, Okta, Google, Amazon, Salesforce, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, CyberArk, PingIdentity, SailPoint, and Dragos. He details how a typical environment would have "Active Directory, they've got some Okta, they've got some Ping Identity, they've got CrowdStrike, they've got Cisco Identity for identity services engine," noting that organizations have "a lot of these kind of identity providers." The slide then transitions to a diagram titled 'Cisco Identity Intelligence,' showing the same logos now uniformly blue with arrows pointing upward to icons labeled 'Users,' 'Machines,' 'Services,' 'Apps,' 'Data,' and 'Behaviors.' Jeetu continues, explaining that "What we've done with Cisco Identity Intelligence is you provide a thin layer of analytics on top of all of those identity providers," utilizing their data in that intelligence layer. The scene shifts to a full-stage view of Jeetu, still speaking and gesturing in front of the 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' diagram, before the presentation transitions to a complex 'IDENTITY GRAPH' visualization. He states, "And what we do is we create an identity graph," further elaborating that it "essentially allows you to correlate data from humans or users and devices and applications." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.
segments.visual_original_caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a dimly lit stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small black device in his hand and gestures expressively as he speaks, occasionally adjusting his earpiece. The camera cuts to a presentation slide displaying a colorful, overlapping grid of technology company logos including Microsoft, Cisco, Okta, Google, Amazon, Salesforce, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, CyberArk, PingIdentity, SailPoint, and Dragos, set against a dark blue background with abstract light streaks. The slide transitions to a diagram titled 'Cisco Identity Intelligence,' showing the same logos now uniformly blue, with arrows pointing upward to icons labeled 'Users,' 'Machines,' 'Services,' 'Apps,' 'Data,' and 'Behaviors.' The scene then shifts to a full-stage view of Jeetu Patel, who continues to speak and gesture while standing in front of the large screen displaying the 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' diagram.
