segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a presentation slide titled 'Example 1: Continuously assess behavior,' showcasing a detailed IDENTITY GRAPH centered on Jeetu Patel (UID 555192931, San Jose, CA), connected to 'Jeetu’s Macbook Pro' and accessing 'Oracle Customer DB.' A clear, articulate male voice, belonging to Jeetu Patel, explains how "the identity graph comes in and says, 'okay, I can actually have a graduated response, that seems like anomalous behavior,' so I'm going to make sure that we can kill that session." He then transitions to proactive measures, asking, "What about the proactive side?" The visual shifts to 'Example 2: Decommission legacy devices,' updating the graph to show 'Jeetu’s Old ThinkPad' (Windows 7, Mexico City, MX). Jeetu continues, discussing having "a bunch of laptops" and "devices" that he's stopped using, posing the question, "Wouldn't it be nice if I can decommission those legacy devices and not have them always turned on?" He clarifies that "The identity graph can tell you which devices have been dormant and not being utilized and actually automatically give you an idea of decommissioning them." Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, is seen on stage gesturing expressively as the camera pans to reveal a large, curved video screen displaying the IDENTITY GRAPH. The stage is illuminated by vertical blue and purple neon light strips, creating a high-tech, immersive atmosphere. The presentation then moves to 'Example 3: Eliminate outdated permissions,' with the graph updating to show new app connections like 'Zendesk' and 'Salesforce' with a 'Trial' label. Jeetu asks, "What happens if you actually want to eliminate outdated permissions?" and mentions a "proof of concept that you were doing with Zendesk." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a presentation slide titled 'Example 3: Eliminate outdated permissions' under the Cisco Live! branding, showcasing a dynamic IDENTITY GRAPH. This graph centers on a user profile for Jeetu Patel, detailing his UID, role, and location, connected to his Macbook Pro and various services like HRIS, Workday, Zendesk, and Salesforce, with a 'Trial' label on the Salesforce link. As the scene transitions to a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium, Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, stands on a circular stage illuminated by blue and purple neon lights, gesturing expressively. He explains how the system identifies and removes outdated permissions, stating in a clear, confident voice, "What the graph will be able to do is tell you, well, this connection is not being utilized. It's outdated. Let's take away the permissions from it." The large curved video screen above him displays the IDENTITY GRAPH slide, while the audience, many wearing lanyards, watches intently. Cutting to a closer shot, Jeetu continues speaking, holding a small device, emphasizing, "That's the power of the identity graph and that's the power of actually providing the Cisco Identity Intelligence layer." The presentation slide then changes to a colorful background with the text 'Take the Cisco Identity Security Assessment,' which Jeetu introduces by saying, "My ask of you is to make sure that you can take a Cisco Identity Security Assessment which we actually provide to all of you for free." He elaborates that this assessment will reveal issues like improper MFA usage or poor identity hygiene. The camera returns to a wide shot of the professional, high-tech auditorium before cutting back to a medium shot of Jeetu walking across the stage, still gesturing and speaking, encouraging the audience to talk to their account reps if interested.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, stands on a circular stage in a dimly lit auditorium, gesturing expressively while holding a remote. He addresses a seated, engaged audience under blue ambient lighting, flanked by vertical neon light strips. His clear, articulate voice explains, "you'll actually be able to go out and take this identity security assessment so that you can actually be safer because you'll know exactly where the holes are in your system." The curved video screen above him initially displays a colorful slide titled 'Take the Cisco Identity Security Assessment'. He continues, "What we've done is basically had an AI-powered Cisco Security Cloud platform," as the screen transitions to a detailed 'Cisco Security Cloud' diagram, highlighting 'User protection', 'Cloud protection', 'Firewall', and 'Breach protection' layers, with 'AI' at the top, which then updates to 'Identity Intelligence'. Jeetu emphasizes, "we think identity is so important and such a huge threat vector for the future, that we've embedded that all into Cisco Security Cloud." He clarifies that "if you happen to buy a user protection suite... Identity intelligence is included. If you buy the breach protection suite, identity intelligence is included." The screen then shows a new slide titled 'Cisco Identity Intelligence,' available July 2024, listing 'Smart Authentication with Cisco Duo,' 'Smart Access with Cisco Secure Access,' and 'Smart Threat Detection with Cisco XDR.' Jeetu concludes, "if you're buying individual applications, you're buying Duo, you're buying Cisco Secure Access, we will make sure that identity intelligence is also included." Throughout the presentation, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a presentation slide titled 'Cisco Identity Intelligence,' detailing 'Smart Authentication with Cisco Duo,' 'Smart Access with Cisco Secure Access,' and 'Smart Threat Detection with Cisco XDR,' each with an icon and a 'July 2024' availability note, all under the consistent 'Cisco Live!' logo. Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and dark attire, stands on a circular stage illuminated by blue and purple neon strips, holding a device and gesturing expressively. He explains that these products, soon to be available, allow users to work with existing identity providers, providing an 'added layer of intelligence' to prevent identity thefts, which he notes is 'one of the largest concerns that people have.' As he declares, 'So folks, that’s identity intelligence,' the curved video screen above him displays a rainbow-colored graphic with the same text. The audience responds with applause, to which Jeetu expresses, 'Thank you. Thank you.' He then walks across the stage, pausing on a marked black mat in the center aisle, discussing how Cisco aims to be a 'great networking company,' emphasizing that this requires also being a 'great security company.' He highlights the new capabilities being built in and the team's 'hyper-innovation,' acknowledging a past period where their 'innovation velocity had gone down.' Throughout, the camera alternates between close-ups of Jeetu and wide shots of the dimly lit, packed auditorium, maintaining a professional and dynamic atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a presentation slide titled 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability,' detailing 'Delivering insights, actions and outcomes,' with a diagram showing Observability at the center, connected to Networking, Security, and Applications. This transitions to a 'SHIPPING' banner-marked slide listing six 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability use cases,' including Hybrid Application Monitoring and AIOps. Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit with a red patterned pocket square, then appears on a circular stage in a large, dimly lit auditorium, gesturing expressively as his image is projected on massive curved screens behind him. He begins speaking, stating, "One year ago, we stood here in front of you and laid out our full-stack observability strategy." He expresses pride in his team for having "delivered every solution and use case which we talked about." The audience is visible in the foreground under blue ambient lighting, while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains in the top right. A new slide, 'THE NEED,' appears, emphasizing 'Deliver the most optimal and secure experience for employees, partners and customers,' followed by 'Biggest Challenges' like Tool sprawl and High TCO, each with an icon. Ronak continues, explaining that "digital experience has become a key boardroom metric" and that "Cisco AppDynamics and Cisco Observability Platform is helping you achieve your observability goal." He walks across the stage, using hand gestures, as he recounts audience feedback: "you told me loud and clear that you want a unified and a simple observability experience." He further elaborates on the challenge of having "many, many monitoring tools... somewhere between 10 to 140 monitoring tools," concluding that "clearly, this is causing high total cost of ownership." The professional, high-tech atmosphere is maintained by dynamic blue stage lighting and the large screens displaying the presentation content.

segments.integrated_caption: On a circular stage at Cisco Live!, Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, gestures expressively while holding a small device. The stage is bathed in professional blue lighting with vertical neon strips, and a large curved video screen above displays his presentation. He explains how current issues are "causing frictions amongst your op teams" and "delaying your resolution and time to detect," as the screen shows a slide titled 'THE NEED' outlining 'Biggest Challenges' like Tool sprawl, High TCO, and Delayed resolution with corresponding icons. Ronak continues, stating that their "use case driven approach for full stack observability is really resonating with our customers," as the screen transitions to showcase customer testimonials from Vodafone, FAB, and easyJet. He elaborates on "unifying the data, hence reducing the frictions between your op teams, improving the operational efficiency, at the same time reducing the business risk." The camera alternates between close-ups of Ronak and wide shots of the attentive audience. The presentation then highlights Cisco's recognition as a 'Gartner Peer Insights Customers’ Choice 2023' for Application Performance Monitoring & Observability, with Ronak affirming, "Gartner agrees with us. They've recognized us as Customers' Choice, Peer Inside for Observability in 2023." The final slide displayed lists 'Top 4 Cisco Full-Stack Observability use cases,' including SAP Monitoring and AIOps, as Ronak mentions, "We talked about a lot of use cases," and expresses his "pure joy and opportunity for you all to see that live on the show floor." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a presentation slide titled 'SAP monitoring and troubleshooting with Cisco AppDynamics,' marked with a 'SHIPPING' banner, listing three key use cases. A male speaker, Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, begins his presentation, stating, "But here are the four use cases which I really want to kind of spend time today. The first one is around SAP monitoring." The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue ambient lighting, where Ronak stands on a circular white stage, gesturing expressively with a small device in hand. He addresses the attentively watching audience, explaining that "SAP users really want to understand bottleneck into their SAP transactions," aiming for predictive and proactive root cause analysis to avoid outages. He emphasizes the financial impact, stating, "You know one minute of outage on SAP landscape can cost you tens of thousands of dollars," and offers, "So let us help you reduce that pain." As Ronak continues to engage the audience with hand gestures, moving across the stage and pausing on a marked square black mat, the large curved video screen above him transitions from the SAP monitoring slide to a new one titled 'Business Risk Observability,' featuring a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and bullet points for 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' and 'Vulnerability and Threat Intelligence.' He introduces this, saying, "The second use case is around business risk observability. You know security risk assessments are top of the mind for our security ops, IT ops, and application team. And I'm very happy to announce extension..." The stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in blue and purple, creating a high-tech atmosphere, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins with a presentation slide titled 'Business Risk Observability,' prominently announcing 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' with a green banner, while the speaker, Ronak Desai, clearly and confidently explains how this "data security posture management observability" helps "detect, discover, analyze, and quickly take action to protect your data and protect your brand." The slide visually supports his points with a dashboard interface for 'Snowflake_US_East,' displaying database attributes and a table of alerts by severity, alongside bullet points detailing GenAI-powered data security and compliance. As Ronak transitions to "The third use case is around digital experience monitoring," the scene shifts to a 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide, marked with a yellow 'SHIPPING' banner, illustrating a flow from various digital touchpoints to Cisco's monitoring solution, promising "Minutes to triage and recover" and featuring a testimonial from First Abu Dhabi Bank. A wide shot then reveals Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, in a dark suit, gesturing expressively on a circular stage illuminated with blue lighting, addressing an attentive audience seated in rows, with the presentation slides displayed on large curved screens above. He continues, emphasizing the need to understand "every component your application depends upon" to deliver a "flawless digital experience," highlighting Cisco's "unique advantage" in providing "end-to-end holistic understanding" by "ingesting network insight from ThousandEyes and private insight from AppDynamics." The view returns to the 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide before transitioning to a new slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM),' showcasing performance graphs for 'cloudkart-public' as Ronak concludes, "And I am very happy to..." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment begins with a presentation slide titled 'Business Risk Observability,' announcing 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' with a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner. The slide displays a dashboard interface for a 'Database Provider: Snowflake_US_East,' showing attributes like database type, cloud provider (AWS), region (US-EAST-1), and account details, alongside a table of alerts categorized by severity (Critical, Warning), status, category, start time, and alert rules. The slide emphasizes securing traditional and cloud-native applications and workloads, with bullet points for GenAI-powered data security, detecting sensitive data, discovering compliance violations, analyzing root cause, and taking actions. The scene transitions to another slide titled 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring,' marked with a yellow 'SHIPPING' banner, illustrating a flow from various digital touchpoints—Mobile, Browser/Desktop, Gaming apps; API Endpoint, Edge; Network/Internet; Multicloud Services/Apps; Data Center; SaaS Applications/Services—to 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring,' enabling 'Minutes to triage and recover.' A testimonial from First Abu Dhabi Bank highlights a reduction from 5 hours to less than 15 minutes for triage. The camera then cuts to a wide shot of a large auditorium with a circular stage, where a male speaker in a dark suit and white shirt, identified as Ronak Desai, stands holding a small device, gesturing as he presents. The stage is illuminated with blue lighting, and the audience is seated in rows. The presentation slides are displayed on large curved screens above the stage. The scene returns to the 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide, then transitions to a new slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM),' featuring a graph of 'Requests per Minute' and 'End User Response Time Distribution' for a browser application named 'cloudkart-public.' The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a presentation slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM)', featuring a dashboard with graphs for 'Requests per Minute' and 'End User Response Time Distribution'. Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, stands on a circular stage, holding a small device and gesturing expressively. He confidently announces, "we are extending the digital experience monitoring by adding session replay as part of that. And it is going GA this week. It's like Netflix." As he speaks about accessing millions of users and simplifying analytics to quickly narrow down problematic sessions, the camera pulls back to a wide shot, revealing the large, dimly lit auditorium with blue stage lighting and a massive curved screen displaying the presentation slide. Jeetu walks across the stage, engaging with the audience, emphasizing the ease of use: "And it is that simple. Please do go experience that for yourself on the demo floor." He continues, acknowledging the high pressure on IT teams, "pressure on you and your IT team is higher than ever." The presentation then shifts to a new slide announcing 'Cisco AIOps', highlighting 'Unified monitoring', 'Real-time business health', and 'Cross-domain service insights', with a dashboard showing 'Incident Count By Priority' and 'Availability' at 100%. Returning to a medium shot, Jeetu continues to speak and gesture, announcing, "So this week, Cisco AIOps is going live. This is providing you actionable insights to help you improve your IT operation, reducing your mean time to detect and repair." He further explains how it helps streamline incident and change management by focusing on correlated faults and insights. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, speaking expressively on a dimly lit stage. He gestures with his right hand while holding a small device in his left, declaring, "You ask for it and we are delivering it." The camera pulls back to reveal Ronak on a circular platform before a large, dark audience illuminated by blue ambient lighting and flanked by vertical blue and purple neon light strips, creating a high-tech atmosphere. As he continues, the camera cuts to a wide-angle view showing the massive curved video screen above the stage, displaying his image and a presentation slide titled 'GenAI in Cisco AppDynamics and Full-Stack Observability.' Ronak explains that "Monitoring LLM usage by your applications from a cost perspective and an API perspective is already available," aligning with the 'SHIPPING' phase on the slide. He then announces, "we are adding natural language interface on our query language," describing it as "interacting in a human natural language with our tools." He further reveals, "we are going to add autonomous root cause analysis with Cisco AI Assistant for Observability." The presentation transitions to another slide, 'Platform apps & modules, Partner Extensibility' under 'Cisco Observability Platform,' as Ronak emphasizes the platform's uniqueness, stating it's "open, its API driven, and it's extensible," and that this extensibility enables "rapid innovation on this platform" for partners. He concludes by happily announcing "eight new partner modules starting from business insight, ML..." while gesturing towards the screen, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo consistently visible in the top right corner, maintaining a professional, immersive, and tech-focused ambiance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, standing on a dark stage with a deep blue backdrop. But it also means increasing number of changes that is delivered into our production network." She continues, "Only last year, we delivered more than 800,000 changes. That is 19% up from the previous year. And if we continue with this trend, we, I think, can comfortably say that we can easily exceed the 1 million mark this year." The camera alternates between close-ups and medium shots, capturing her expressive delivery. At 0:32, the view widens to reveal Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, standing attentively to her right, holding a small device. Petek addresses "Ronak" and emphasizes HSBC's transformation into a digital bank, stating, "we at tech in technology, what we must really do is have a very sharp focus in protecting our production. And that is only possible by uplifting our technology resilience, which also includes investing in observability and AIOps." A distinct "boing" sound effect punctuates her final words. Jeetu Patel, whose voice is now heard, immediately responds, "That's not me." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, featuring a circular stage illuminated by spotlights and surrounded by a vast audience. On stage, Petek Ergul, a woman with a blonde bob, black-rimmed glasses with white temples, a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and a distinctive geometric pendant necklace, stands facing Ronak Desai, a man in a dark suit with a white shirt and red patterned pocket square, holding a small device. Ronak gestures expressively as he speaks, his clear voice discussing the bank's role in the global economy and asserting that "security and uptime, I'm sure, is not negotiable." He then directly asks Petek, "So what does technology resilience mean to HSBC? How do you approach it specifically in context of observability?" Petek listens attentively, and the camera cuts to a close-up of her as she begins to speak, her expression animated and her hands gesturing to emphasize her points, her image simultaneously displayed on the large screens behind the stage. With a clear and articulate voice, Petek affirms, "You're absolutely spot on, resilience is not negotiable." She explains that as a bank, HSBC's "license to operate very much rests on trust," encompassing customers, staff, regulators, and the public, assuring the audience, "we take this responsibility very, very seriously," which is why they adopt "a very proactive approach to technology resilience." She continues, detailing that as part of HSBC's Vision 2027 technology strategy, they "developed a very powerful and very unique tool for our industry," which is a "self-assessment tool called Technology Resilience Maturity Framework," or TRMF. The camera alternates between close-ups of Petek and wider shots showing both speakers on the stage, capturing their interaction, all while the professional and engaging atmosphere, the audience in darkness, the 'Cisco Live!' logo, and the blue and white lighting maintain a modern, tech-focused ambiance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, speaking passionately on stage at Cisco Live!. Dressed in a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and distinctive black-rimmed glasses with white temples, she gestures expressively with her hands. She articulates how their approach helps "identify, probably previously unknown faults or problem areas," allowing them to "proactively prevent incidents" and "detect and restore instance much faster," while also providing "insights to much better, more accurate ways to identify root causes for any incidents." The camera pulls back to reveal Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, standing opposite her, holding notes and a small device. He responds with animated hand gestures, asking, "So in business terms for HSBC, what does this consistent and unified approach to absolutely mean to you, your teams and to all our customers here?" Petek then replies, "Yes. So first of all, observability must ensure the technology teams are more aligned and more focused on customer outcomes." She continues, explaining it "should really closely tied into the service defined service level objectives," and that "over many years, we have made significant progress uplifting our monitoring, alerting and logging capabilities by introduction of..." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, standing on a circular stage at Cisco Live!. He holds a small device in one hand and gestures expressively as he speaks into a headset microphone, his voice clear and articulate, explaining that "98% of meetings that you'll see in the future will have at least one participant that's going to be not in the same physical location as everyone else." The camera pulls back to reveal a large, dimly lit auditorium, the Innovation Theater, filled with an attentive audience, illuminated by dynamic blue stage lighting and vertical neon light strips. Above, a large curved video screen displays Jeetu’s image and presentation slides, transitioning from 'Reimagining workspaces' to 'Distance Zero: Making separation a thing of the past,' showing a modern conference room. Jeetu walks across the marked stage area, engaging the audience with dynamic hand movements, emphasizing that "only a third of the conference rooms in the world are actually video enabled." He passionately introduces the concept of "distance zero" as a core principle for "innovating in this new world of hybrid work," explaining it's about fundamentally taking away the distance between parties not in the same physical space, and clarifying that it's "not just about putting a video and a screen on every room." The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the professional and immersive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, stands on the circular, blue-lit stage at Cisco Live!, holding a small device and speaking into a headset microphone with expressive gestures. The large curved video screen behind him initially displays 'Distance Zero: Making separation a thing of the past' with a modern conference room image. He articulates with a clear, engaging voice about fundamentally architecting rooms to take away distance, providing "blueprints for every different kind of room" to customers, including boardrooms, huddle spaces, theater-style rooms, and conference rooms, to ensure a level of distance can be removed so people feel immersed. The screen then transitions to a full-screen presentation slide titled 'Distance Zero in any meeting, in any room,' showcasing four 3D renderings of diverse meeting setups equipped with Cisco collaboration technology. Jeetu continues, emphasizing how people feel "right there with each other," even noting that sometimes you "don't even remember if you've met someone in person or not" because the distance is taken away. With dynamic hand movements, he introduces a "complete portfolio of devices" that Cisco has built for "every single space" they want to equip with the 'Distance Zero' notion. The screen behind him shifts to a slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices,' displaying a lineup of Cisco products like Desk Pro, Board Pro 55/75, Room Kit EQX, Room Bar, Room Bar Pro, Room Kit EQ, Room Kit Pro, Room Navigator, Desk Camera 4K, Headset 320 Series, and Headset 720 Series, complete with a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge. He concludes by highlighting the fascinating innovation in these devices, as they "completely changed our mind-set." The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event's branding throughout this professional and engaging presentation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He holds a small device and gestures expressively while speaking into a headset microphone, articulating a strategic shift from devices working "only the Webex platform" to supporting "any platform that customers are using." He specifically mentions opening up their devices to work with "Zoom" and "Google," and highlights a "special partnership with Microsoft" so that "Microsoft Team Rooms can run natively on our devices." As he speaks, the camera pans across the large, dimly lit auditorium, showing hundreds of attentive attendees in business attire. A massive curved video screen behind him, illuminated with blue lighting, displays a presentation slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices,' showcasing Cisco collaboration products like Desk Pro, Board Pro, Room Kit series, Room Bar, Room Navigator, Desk Camera 4K, and Headsets, complete with a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge. Jeetu continues, noting that when Microsoft Team Rooms run natively, "Webex can be another application that runs there." He then discusses a decision made "five or six years ago" to ensure "every one of our devices would have an Nvidia chipset in it" to "pack it with capabilities for AI," which is now "really proving out to be extremely valuable." The Cisco Live! logo is consistently visible, reinforcing the professional and engaging atmosphere of the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He holds a small device and gestures expressively into a headset microphone, explaining that Cisco's "AI capabilities are not just in our devices, but those capabilities for AI can make every other platform that integrates with our devices better." He specifically notes that "if you actually use Microsoft Teams, they will get benefit of noise removal and noise cancellation and video intelligence, because that's something that's baked into the devices." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the large auditorium, showing the expansive stage with multiple large screens displaying a slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices,' listing Cisco collaboration products like Desk Pro, Board Pro 55/75, Room Kit EQX, and Headset 720 Series, along with certification dates and a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge. Jeetu continues to speak, moving across the stage and using hand gestures, emphasizing that "it's not just about collaboration and hybrid work," but about taking "the power of the entirety of the portfolio of Cisco." The presentation transitions to a new slide titled 'Deliver workspace optimization and sustainability with Cisco Spaces,' which displays a detailed 3D floor plan of the Meraki San Francisco SFO12 5th Floor, showing real-time data on occupancy, indoor air quality, and desk usage. Jeetu explains that this capability allows them "to deliver workspace optimization through Cisco Spaces," taking "telemetry from Meraki Wi-Fi access points and IoT devices" to provide "a schematic of exactly what your space looks like, what spaces are available," and also taking "the telemetry from the devices." He concludes by stating, "that's reimagining workspaces themselves." Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner, and the dynamic blue lighting illuminates the stage and audience.

segments.integrated_caption: At Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer and black shirt, confidently walks across a circular white stage, gesturing expressively while speaking into a headset microphone. The stage is illuminated by vertical blue and purple neon lights, with the audience visible under ambient blue lighting. A large curved video screen above displays 'Reimagining work' as Jeetu states, "We also wanted to make sure that we fundamentally reimagine work." He continues, emphasizing the need for a common platform where innovation is available in every application. The screen then transitions to showcase the 'Webex Suite' with a gradient pink-to-purple toggle switch, listing features like Meetings, Messaging, Calling, Webinars, Events, Whiteboarding, Polling, and Async Video, each with a corresponding icon. Jeetu articulates that the Webex Suite includes "calling, messaging, meetings, polling, Q&A, events, whiteboarding, async video, all of those things for the price less than the cost of Netflix." He highlights its single platform, full integration, gorgeous UI, and complete security, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event’s branding.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jeetu Patel stands on a circular white stage under dynamic blue stage lighting, addressing a packed audience. A female voiceover begins, announcing, "Enter the era of spatial computing, Webex for Apple Vision Pro." This accompanies a digital demonstration of Webex for Apple Vision Pro, showcasing a futuristic living room environment with floating app icons like Apple TV, Music, and Webex. The Webex interface is displayed in a spatial computing context, with multiple participants appearing in floating video windows, engaging in a collaborative meeting. The voiceover continues, describing it as a space "where every space is your favorite workspace" with an "infinite canvas" for collaboration, leveraging "industry-leading features like real-time translation and AI noise removal." The scene then cuts back to Jeetu Patel on stage, wearing a dark blazer, black shirt, and clear-framed glasses, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively while speaking into a headset microphone. He announces, "Available in the App Store today," which is met with enthusiastic applause from the attentive audience. The large curved video screen behind him displays 'Apple + Cisco Better together'. Jeetu continues, expressing excitement about the Apple partnership and emphasizing Cisco's commitment to investing in the partnership and building amazing products with privacy and security at the forefront. The segment concludes with a return to the Webex demonstration, highlighting seamless integration, followed by a final screen displaying the Webex by Cisco logo on a vibrant purple background.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and dark attire, stands on a circular white stage under a massive curved video screen initially displaying 'Apple + Cisco Better together'. The audience is bathed in blue ambient lighting. Jeetu, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively with a headset microphone, walks dynamically across the stage, engaging the crowd. He speaks about being "completely immersed into it," stating, "that's Apple," before posing the question, "How do you also make sure that you don't just reimagine workspaces and work, but also customer experience?" As he continues, the screen transitions to a slide titled 'Delivering great customer experiences', detailing 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement' connected by the 'Webex Platform'. Jeetu emphasizes leveraging "that same platform that we've used for video and audio, um, for Webex," to build "world-class customer experience set of applications." He explains how the core Webex platform enables not just reactive customer service but also "proactive communications," where "the customer shouldn't even have to call because something's gone wrong," and the brand can "proactively make sure that you take care of that customer." He adds the importance of providing "every customer the ability to self-serve." The camera alternates between close-ups of Jeetu and wide shots of the stage and engaged audience, highlighting the scale of the event, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner amidst dynamic blue and green neon lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively with his free hand, speaking into a headset microphone. His clear, confident voice explains, "there is such a thing as wanting to have the desire to talk to a human from time to time," emphasizing that "we want to make sure that you don't have to keep repeating yourself, you don't have to keep feeling frustrated, and you can actually talk to the human and get your problem solved as easily as possible." As he walks across the stage, the camera pulls back to reveal the full auditorium with seated attendees and a large curved screen above, displaying a 'Webex Platform' slide with 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement'. Jeetu continues, "you might start that communication with that brand on a website, in chat, and then you might decide to jump on a voice call," highlighting that "we will actually keep complete context of that, and all of those multiple channels, whether it be WhatsApp or websites or Instagram or a telephone call or a video call, will all actually maintain context." He points towards the screen and audience with dynamic hand movements, reinforcing his points about not having to repeat oneself. The stage, illuminated with blue and green vertical light strips, and the minimalist design keep the focus on Jeetu, who concludes, "we've actually made these solutions available for everyone," mentioning a past "standard and premium version which was the feature-rich contact center." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible, and the attentive audience watches as Jeetu delivers his energetic and informative presentation.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, stands on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively into a headset microphone. He announces a "contact center basic" solution, explaining in a clear, engaging voice that it's designed for "smaller, more tactical teams that might not need a very heavy-duty contact center," and highlights that "that's included in the Webex Suite." Behind him, a massive curved video screen displays a 'Solutions for everyone' slide, visually reinforcing his words with the 'Basic' tier labeled 'INCLUDED in Webex Suite' for 'Smaller, tactical teams.' He elaborates that purchasing the Webex Suite provides this basic contact center, offering multi-line communication and features beneficial for IT help desks or HR ticketing systems. As he moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated hand movements, he then declares, "And so what we've done is we've said, let's take it a step further." He proceeds to announce "the Essentials version," which he states will be available for "a much lower price than the full-fledged standard version for $30 per user per month." The 'Essentials' tier on the screen aligns perfectly, showing 'Customer-facing teams (any size)' and the '30 USD per user per month' price. He concludes by emphasizing that this tier empowers "any individual within your organization that can actually participate in helping solve problems for customers." The camera alternates between wide shots of the engaged audience and closer shots of Jeetu, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, dynamically presenting on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!'s dimly lit auditorium. Blue and green ambient lighting illuminates the stage and vertical neon strips, while an attentive audience watches. Jeetu, holding a small black device and speaking into a headset microphone, gestures expressively as he explains that "AI is pervasive in every aspect of the Webex Platform," a statement mirrored on the large curved video screen above him. He elaborates on how "all of this is built on a core fabric of AI," emphasizing that "AI is pervasive throughout the entirety of the platform." As he speaks, the screen transitions to a diagram illustrating the 'AI Assistant's' integration across the Webex ecosystem, including Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, Webex Control Hub, and Webex Contact Center. Jeetu continues with enthusiasm, detailing their goal to "make sure that we have once again a unified AI assistant for all of the properties of Webex," specifically mentioning "Webex Suite," "Cisco Devices," and "Webex Control Hub" for managing consoles. The screen then displays the title 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Suite' on a vibrant blue-to-orange gradient. Jeetu confirms this, stating, "that AI assistant is going to be called the Cisco AI Assistant for the Webex Suite," before excitedly promising, "Let me show you a quick demo that's going to blow your mind. This is very cool." Throughout his animated delivery and strong eye contact with the audience, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, stands on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively into a headset microphone. He enthusiastically explains a Webex feature, setting up a scenario where he steps away from a meeting because his doorbell rings. As he speaks, the scene transitions to a screen demonstration of the Webex platform, showing a multi-person video meeting. The text 'Be right back' appears, and a notification pops up: 'We detected that you've stepped away from the meeting. You're muted and your video is off,' accompanied by a steaming coffee cup icon, visually illustrating Jeetu's narrative about a participant named Clarissa stepping away. Upon the participant's return, a recap notification appears: 'Catch up on what you've missed while you were away from the meeting,' followed by a summary of discussion points, which Jeetu highlights as a key benefit. The audience responds with applause to his rhetorical question about the feature's coolness. The video then cuts back to a wide shot of Jeetu on stage, continuing his presentation in front of a large, curved screen displaying the recap interface, with the audience visible in the dimly lit auditorium. He emphasizes that this summarization capability extends to the messaging platform and Vidcast, before the video concludes with close-ups of Jeetu, gesturing as he elaborates on the Webex AI Assistant's features, which are also shown on the curved screen with slides like 'AI is pervasive in every aspect of the Webex Platform' and 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Suite'.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! under dynamic blue and green lighting. He holds a small black device, gestures expressively, and speaks into a headset microphone, engaging the visible audience. Jeetu's voice explains that the transcript will capture more than just spoken words, stating, "you will even know in the transcript on what transpired besides just the um um just the script... the transcript will have Clarissa stepped away, there was an emoji, there were people that applauded. All of those non-verbal cues will actually be something that'll be available in the transcript for you." This aligns with a close-up visual of a digital 'Transcript' interface on a large screen, showing real-time meeting notes like 'Clarissa steps away...' and emoji reactions. The camera then pulls back to reveal Jeetu speaking in front of a massive curved video screen that mirrors this interface, displaying a detailed transcript of a marketing meeting. Jeetu continues, expressing excitement about AI capabilities, noting, "you should expect these to be at a very high velocity of innovation that'll continue to keep getting launched and delivered to you." The scene cuts to a vibrant title slide, 'Reimagining the IT experience with AI,' before returning to Jeetu, who continues his presentation center stage with vertical blue light strips in the background, emphasizing, "it's not just for the end user. We also care about the IT administrator in making their lives easier with AI... we make their experience magical with AI." The final frames show a new slide titled 'Bringing everything together for IT,' outlining a comprehensive IT ecosystem, while the Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a presentation slide titled 'Bringing everything together for IT,' which outlines a comprehensive IT ecosystem, including Webex Control Hub, Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, Webex Contact Center, an Analytics Platform, and a Data Lake with various data sources. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue ambient lighting, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt, stands on a circular white stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks into a headset microphone. The massive curved video screen behind him displays the same 'Bringing everything together for IT' slide, and his image is mirrored on side screens. Jeetu Patel articulates that 'everything together for IT' is achieved by having a common data lake platform, an analytics platform, and then Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, and Webex Contact Center, all managed in 'one single administrative management plane, which is called the Webex Control Hub.' He emphasizes that Control Hub is not just one of the best in the collaboration industry but also in enterprise software, making it hard to find 'amazing management tools like Control Hub.' The camera cuts to a close-up of Jeetu, who holds a small black device and continues to gesture, explaining that the audience is seeing an example of Control Hub, where everything can be managed from a single location. The presentation continues with a new slide titled 'Webex Control Hub,' showing a user interface with an 'Overview' section indicating 'Total engaged users: 4500' and a graph of user engagement. Jeetu then reveals they've 'integrated that with network performance insights with Meraki,' as the next slide, 'Network performance insights with Meraki,' displays a detailed dashboard with client connection diagrams and signal quality graphs. He concludes by stating, 'let's take it a step further,' introducing 'the Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub,' as the final slide, 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Control Hub,' appears with a vibrant gradient background. Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains in the top right corner, and the attentive audience is visible in the darkened auditorium.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, black shirt, and clear-framed glasses, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He holds a small black device and gestures expressively while speaking into a headset microphone, his voice clear as he asks, "So what does that do exactly? What would you do with the Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub?" He then explains a scenario: "Let's say that you actually had word from someone as an IT administrator that this senior executive Donna Miller in your organization has actually been having some bad quality issues for some reason." The video transitions to a screen recording of the Webex Control Hub interface, displaying an 'Overview' dashboard with user engagement metrics like 'Total engaged users: 4512'. The 'Cisco AI Assistant' panel appears on the right, greeting the user, and Jeetu continues, explaining how one can "just simply ask the AI Assistant, 'Hey, how is Donna Miller's quality?'" or "'Show me any issues that she's having.'" The interface then shows the AI Assistant responding to the query "Show me any meeting issues for Donna Miller," confirming that "during a meeting, Donna Miller had some choppy audio" due to a 31% device audio packet loss. Jeetu emphasizes that the assistant goes a step further, showing "exactly where that choppy audio is" with a detailed 'Meeting diagnostics' view, displaying a timeline of the audio quality issue and a network path visualization with metrics like latency and jitter. He concludes by stating, "We integrate that with ThousandEyes, so ThousandEyes is then going to be able to provide you with details," as the interface continues to display the diagnostic information, pinpointing the problem to a specific network segment.
segments.visual_original_caption: The segment begins with Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and clear-framed glasses, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He holds a small black device in one hand and gestures expressively with the other while speaking into a headset microphone. The background is a dark blue curtain, and the 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right corner. As he speaks, the video transitions to a screen recording of the Webex Control Hub interface. The interface displays an 'Overview' dashboard with metrics such as 'Total engaged users: 4512' and user engagement categories like 'Power users,' 'Core users,' and 'Casual users.' On the right side, the 'Cisco AI Assistant' panel appears, greeting the user with 'Hi there!' and offering to guide through Control Hub tasks. The assistant is then queried with 'Show me any meeting issues for Donna Miller,' and it responds with a summary indicating potential choppy audio during her last meeting due to a 31% device audio packet loss. The assistant further provides a detailed 'Meeting diagnostics' view, showing a timeline of the audio quality issue and a network path visualization with metrics like latency and jitter, pinpointing the problem to a specific network segment. The interface remains the focus for the remainder of the segment, demonstrating the AI Assistant's diagnostic capabilities.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the Webex Control Hub interface, showcasing the Cisco AI Assistant's network diagnostic alert for user Donna Miller, which indicates a 31% audio packet loss exceeding the 8% threshold. A ThousandEyes visualization highlights the 'Local ISP network' as the problematic segment, while Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt, speaks into a headset microphone from a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He explains that "the network path is actually going to show you why that issue occurred," confirming that "in this particular case it was a local ISP that caused the issue," though it could also be "the app," "your device," or "the network." He emphasizes the system's ability to "pinpoint exactly where that issue is for the network administrator," noting the "beauty about all of this is I'm asking questions in natural language and I'm getting back responses in charts." The scene transitions to a wider shot of Jeetu on the dynamically lit stage, gesturing expressively to the audience, as the presentation slides shift to 'Sustainability'. He announces, "Another big area that we actually focused on is sustainability," and explains that Control Hub will "add more capabilities for sustainability," specifically announcing "a new low carbon mode for sustainability." The curved video screens above him display a dashboard with device energy consumption metrics (309K kWh, 72 tCO2e) and a list of features including 'New! Low Carbon Mode' highlighted in green. The camera pulls back further, revealing the full scale of the Innovation Theater with its large screens and engaged audience, as Jeetu concludes, "So folks, that's the entirety of the collaboration platform, tremendous amount of innovation in both..."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment begins with a close-up of the Webex Control Hub interface, specifically the Cisco AI Assistant, displaying a network diagnostic alert for user Donna Miller. The alert indicates a 31% audio packet loss exceeding the 8% threshold for three consecutive minutes, with a detailed network path visualization from ThousandEyes highlighting the 'Local ISP network' as the problematic segment. The interface includes options to set up a live meeting alert and a feedback prompt. The scene then transitions to Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. The camera pulls back to reveal the full stage setup, including large curved screens displaying the speaker and the presentation content, emphasizing the scale of the event and the professional, tech-focused atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, energetically walking across a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He gestures expressively with his hands while speaking into a headset microphone, holding a small black device, as he highlights Cisco's extensive capabilities across networking, security, observability, and collaboration, expressing excitement to bring these to the audience. The stage is illuminated with vertical blue and purple neon light strips, and the camera pans to show the vast, dimly lit audience, bathed in blue light, before cutting back to Jeetu as he announces he's handing over to his 'good friend Oliver.' As Jeetu concludes, applause erupts from the engaged audience, followed by upbeat, energetic background music. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, then walks onto the stage with a warm demeanor, gesturing openly. He thanks Jeetu and all the speakers, passionately declaring Cisco's offering as 'the best portfolio ever.' Oliver then transitions to discuss the crucial topic of sustainability, acknowledging it as a challenging project but assuring the audience of support from a dedicated team of over 6,000 service experts. He moves confidently across the stage, occasionally pausing to emphasize points, with the same vertical neon lights in the background, as the camera captures wide shots of the stage and the attentive audience. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the professional and immersive presentation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, standing on a dark stage illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue. He gestures expressively with both hands raised, then clasps them together as he speaks, his voice introducing "my colleague Adele Trombetta, who's running customer experience and service teams all around the mirror to explain you how she can help you." He moves dynamically across the stage before walking off to the right. The camera follows as Adele Trombetta, a woman with short brown hair, glasses, and a light beige blazer over a turtleneck, walks onto the circular white stage from the left, gesturing with her right hand as she approaches the center. The camera pulls back to reveal a large, curved video screen behind her displaying her name and title: 'Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco'. The audience, visible in the darkened auditorium, erupts in applause and cheering. The screen then transitions to a vibrant, animated graphic of a green, tree-like network. Adele stands center stage, speaking with an animated expression and gesturing with open hands, her voice clear as she greets the audience: "Hello, hello everyone, it's great to be here with you all." She then introduces her topic, "So now, topic is sustainability. You have heard a lot about that, but the point is, where do we start? How do we start? Well, we start with an X-ray." She continues, explaining, "I know it may sound strange, but before X-ray, what happened underneath the human body was invisible to the naked eye. With X-ray, suddenly the invisible became visible. And this is exactly how we should manage sustainability. Why? Because not everybody has visibility into today's sustainability performance. Actually, we've done a study with IDC, and..." A lower-third graphic appears, confirming her name and title. The camera alternates between close-ups of Adele and wide shots of the stage, showing the audience and the large screen displaying her live feed alongside the animated background. Blue spotlights illuminate the stage, creating a professional and immersive atmosphere, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo remaining in the top right corner throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA at Cisco, stands confidently on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, dressed in a light beige double-breasted blazer over a turtleneck, dark trousers, and white heels. She gestures expressively with her hands as she speaks, her clear, articulate voice discussing the low percentage of IT departments in EMEA (only 18%) that have defined and track sustainability baselines, emphasizing the critical need for visibility to act, especially with new EU initiatives. The stage is illuminated with cool blue and purple vertical light strips, and the camera alternates between medium close-ups of Adele and wide shots of the large, dimly lit auditorium, showing the engaged audience. As Adele states, "Now, when you run an X-ray, you may find some challenges," the massive curved video screen above the stage transitions to a slide titled 'Challenges,' listing '1. Translating data into insights,' '2. Adopting the right technologies,' and '3. Managing budget pressure,' set against a background of dry, cracked earth. The screen also displays a live feed of Adele as she elaborates on these points, mentioning the lack of tools, automation, and data foundation, the need for help with technology to evolve business performance and sustainability goals, and budget struggles. The camera then pulls back to a wide-angle view, revealing the full scale of the venue, the circular stage, and the audience, as Adele concludes by stating, "But consider that 91% of the surveyed business in EMEA..." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently in the top right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco, stands on a circular white stage, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple lighting. Dressed in a light beige double-breasted blazer, turtleneck, dark trousers, and white heels, with short brown hair and glasses, Adele speaks passionately and articulately about sustainability. Behind her, a massive curved video screen initially displays 'Turning challenges into opportunities' over green sprouts. As the camera zooms in, a 'Live from the Keynote' banner appears in the top left, and Adele gestures expressively, emphasizing her points about achieving "profitable growth from daily sustainability initiatives" and aligning "technology and sustainability goals." She asserts that "sustainability is great for business too" and that the difference lies in "how we use the technology," expressing deep passion for both sustainability and the audience's success. She highlights Cisco's technology, including Silicon One and AI, as tools to help achieve these goals. The presentation then transitions to a slide titled 'Data centers,' which visually details sustainability goals of "Reduce 40% power capacity" and business performance of "Reduce 23% operational expenses," accompanied by server racks, as Adele verbally reinforces these exact figures: "Your data center with 40% less power capacity and 23% less operational cost." This is swiftly followed by a 'Smart buildings' slide, showing "Reduce 50% power consumption" and "Reduce 50% power costs + Improved employee experience" with skyscrapers, which Adele also echoes, mentioning "your offices with 50% less power consumption and cost." Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains in the top right, and the atmosphere is professional and engaging, with a focused audience attentively watching her animated delivery.

segments.integrated_caption: Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA at Cisco, stands on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, dressed in a light beige double-breasted blazer, turtleneck, dark trousers, and white heels. She speaks expressively, using hand gestures, her clear voice emphasizing points about 'employee experience' and how Cisco is 'digitizing the grid across most of the European countries.' Behind her, a large curved video screen initially displays a 'Smart buildings' slide, highlighting sustainability goals to 'Reduce 50% power consumption' and business performance goals to 'Reduce 50% power costs + Improved employee experience,' set against modern skyscrapers and green trees. Adele asserts these are 'real and proven savings' that Cisco is ready to deliver. As she poses the question, 'Now the big question is, how do you do that?', the slide transitions to 'Your sustainability journey,' depicting a winding mountain road with 'Shape,' 'Scale,' and 'Sustain' labeled, reinforcing her message that 'there is no one journey fits all' because 'you are unique' and so is 'your sustainability journey.' She then introduces three steps, stating, 'The first is shape,' at which point the screen displays a detailed diagram titled 'Shape your sustainable technology roadmap,' outlining organizational and technology readiness, including strategy, governance, execution, IT for sustainable business, sustainable IT, enterprise IT, value streams, and ESG data, with sub-components like 'Vision and objectives,' 'Compliance,' 'Energy management,' and 'Reporting and dashboards.' The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently in the top right corner. The audience is visible in the dimly lit Innovation Theater, seated under blue lighting, as the camera alternates between wide shots of the stage and closer views of Adele, maintaining a professional and focused atmosphere with dynamic lighting and clear visual aids.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a detailed presentation slide titled 'Shape your sustainable technology roadmap,' outlining a comprehensive framework for sustainability, as Adele Trombetta's articulate voice begins, explaining that "in third, we can build an actionable roadmap." The scene transitions to a vibrant, split-screen graphic titled 'Scale your transformation,' showcasing 'Data centers' with server racks, 'Smart buildings' with green trees and modern architecture, and 'Industry solutions' with wind turbines. Adele continues, stating the "second step is scale," emphasizing bringing together global expertise to "improve your energy consumption with real-time observability, digitize your workplace and automate the most critical processes in many industries." The camera then pulls back to reveal Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco, standing confidently in a light beige double-breasted blazer on a circular stage, her image mirrored on the massive curved screen behind her. She gestures expressively as she speaks, introducing the final stage: "Last is sustain. You have to sustain your journey," and highlighting the use of "amazing and digital dashboards that will give you real-time data, insights, and recommendations." This aligns with the presentation slide titled 'Sustain your journey with actionable insights,' displaying a dashboard with metrics for Power usage, Carbon emissions, and Recommendations. After Adele invites the audience to "hear from our customers" and asks to "run the video," the scene briefly cuts to a close-up of cracked, dry earth, symbolizing environmental challenges. This transitions to a new speaker, Petek Ergul, CTO, Global Head of Service Management at HSBC, who appears in a professional office setting, wearing a tan blazer and distinctive geometric pendant necklace. She speaks directly to the camera with a clear, professional voice, stating that "Automation will deliver better customer experiences, operational efficiencies, and help us to achieve our net zero energy goals within Vision 27." The Cisco Live! branding remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a blue shirt and glasses, seated in a modern office, as a female voice states, "Now we're working together with Cisco to drive this transformation with a greater purpose," echoing the on-screen text. The scene transitions to a woman in a green blazer speaking passionately in a minimalist room, with text indicating, 'We are working with Cisco on our sustainability strategy'. Umar Ahmed, Director, Global Head of Network Architecture at Deutsche Bank, then speaks directly to the camera in a well-lit office, elaborating, "We are working with Cisco on our sustainability strategy for our global data centers. With their expertise, we will reduce our carbon footprint, more importantly, increase our usage of renewable energy." This is followed by a group of four professionals in a conference room discussing around a table, visually reinforcing the theme of increasing renewable energy usage, before a man in a gray sweater is shown typing on a laptop outdoors with a digital overlay of green foliage. A group of six people are then seen in a casual meeting on gray sofas as a male voice explains, "Working with CX has been important to make the concept of smart buildings repeatable and sustainable." Colin Bannon, CTO of BT, speaks in a modern office, stating, "We're looking at over 70% less electricity to run 10X worth of performance," then appears on a rooftop overlooking a cityscape, concluding, "And that's better for sustainability, it's better for our costs." The scene transitions to a futuristic outdoor structure with solar panels, where a group of three people walk and converse, with text stating, 'Cisco is part of our family.' Eman Al Awadhi, Vice President, Network & Cyber Security at Expo City Technology, speaks in a modern office, affirming, "Cisco is part of Expo's family. It was a fusion of shared values and expertise, demonstrating our agility and commitment to a sustainable solution." The video then cuts to an aerial view of a lush green island with a river and mountains, followed by a nighttime shot of a massive, illuminated Cisco-branded building. The final scenes show a large, dark auditorium with a circular stage, where a woman in a beige blazer speaks to a massive audience, accompanied by applause, and she addresses "Oliver," highlighting "these customers are amazing sustainability change makers. And every year at Cisco Live..." The video concludes with a graphic showcasing six individuals from various companies, each with a sustainability-related achievement, all under the 'Cisco Live!' branding, as the speaker's voice trails off with "Cisco Live!"

The camera then cuts to Umar Ahmed, Director, Global Head of Network Architecture at Deutsche Bank, speaking directly to the camera in a well-lit office, with the same sustainability text overlay. The scene shifts to a group of four professionals in a conference room with large windows, discussing around a table with laptops, as the text 'and, more importantly, increase our usage of renewable energy' is displayed. A man in a gray sweater is shown typing on a laptop outdoors, with a digital overlay of green foliage, reinforcing the sustainability theme. The video then shows a group of six people in a casual meeting on gray sofas, with the text 'the concept of smart buildings repeatable and sustainable.' Next, Colin Bannon, CTO of BT, speaks in a modern office, stating 'We’re looking at over 70 percent less electricity,' followed by a shot of him standing on a rooftop overlooking a cityscape, with the text 'And that’s better for our costs.' The scene transitions to a futuristic outdoor structure with solar panels, where a group of three people walk and converse, with the text 'Cisco is part of our family.' Eman Al Awadhi, Vice President, Network & Cyber Security at Expo City Technology, speaks in a modern office, with the text 'demonstrating our agility and commitment to a sustainable solution.' The video then cuts to an aerial view of a lush green island with a river and mountains, followed by a nighttime shot of a massive, illuminated Cisco-branded building. The final scenes show a large, dark auditorium with a circular stage, where a woman in a beige blazer speaks to a massive audience, with large screens displaying speaker profiles. The video concludes with a graphic showcasing six individuals from various companies, each with a sustainability-related achievement, such as 'Enabling a green future' and 'Efficient automated operations,' all under the 'Cisco Live!' branding.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dark auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Adele Trombetta, a woman in a beige blazer, stands on a circular white stage alongside Oliver Tuszik. Behind them, a large curved screen displays 'Cisco Lifecycle Services' and its tagline 'Simple, flexible, outcomes-focused,' with a scrolling list of benefits. Adele gestures expressively as she speaks, confidently stating, "6,000 experts in my team and tens of thousands expert with our partners, we have got your back and we are ready to start. Let's go." The screen then transitions to a bold, repeating message: 'WE'VE GOT YOUR BACK' in bright blue text. As the camera pulls back to reveal the vast audience and the immersive stage setup with blue lighting, Adele exits. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit, takes center stage, walking confidently while gesturing and holding a small device. He speaks passionately about customers, saying, "I want to end this session with a customer close to me... this is a special one... you will understand in a second why he's a special one." The lighting shifts to include green and yellow accents as he introduces, "Please welcome on stage, Cisco's CIO, Fletcher Previn!" Energetic music plays as a man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer, walks onto the stage from the side. Oliver greets him, "So, it's good also to have you on stage, finally," and Fletcher responds, "Thanks for having me." The two men greet each other warmly, shaking hands and engaging in conversation. The stage is illuminated with dynamic lighting, and large screens display their images and presentation content, including Fletcher Previn’s name and title, creating an energetic and professional atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit, speaks animatedly on a circular white stage, gesturing with both hands. He addresses Fletcher Previn, who listens attentively in a dark suit, asking in a distinct non-native English accent about their role as 'customer zero' for new ideas, specifically introducing the topic of AI infrastructure. The camera pulls back to reveal the massive curved screen above the stage displaying Fletcher Previn’s name and title. Close-ups show Oliver continuing to speak with expressive hand movements and a warm smile, before Fletcher Previn, identified by a lower-third graphic as 'SVP and Chief Information Officer, Cisco', begins to speak with hands clasped. In a clear American accent, Fletcher explains the 'customer zero' concept, highlighting the challenge and excitement of doing things for the first time. He then details the need to build a high-performance AI cluster of GPUs using the Cisco Ethernet stack, aiming to prove its capability, create shared infrastructure across the company, and develop a reusable blueprint, mentioning the use of RDMA. The scene transitions to a detailed technical slide titled 'Deployment Architecture', illustrating a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)' with various Cisco and SONiC components, including storage, compute, and management networks, branded with 'Cisco Live!' and a 2024 copyright notice, visually supporting Fletcher's technical discussion.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a detailed technical slide from Cisco Live! titled 'Deployment Architecture', showcasing a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)'. A male host in a dark suit, speaking with a clear and articulate voice, immediately begins to describe the diagram, explaining the connection between GPUs and the cluster. He details the 400-gig leaf and spine network with 32 nodes and 256 Nvidia H100 GPUs, connected by 400-gig optical links to a 100-gig storage network and a 100-gig in-band network. He further elaborates on the control plane and the 10-gig connectivity for out-of-band management, mentioning Cisco ISRs for out-of-band, Nexus 9Ks, and Cisco 8000s as the Ethernet backbone. He humorously notes that as a Cisco employee, he's allowed to 'over-provision'. The scene then transitions to a live stage presentation where the host, gesturing expressively, stands alongside a bearded man in a gray suit (Oliver Tuszik) before a large audience under dark blue and purple stage lighting, with the Cisco Live! logo visible. The video then cuts to another slide titled 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster', displaying six images of Cisco 8000 series Silicon One devices, Nvidia DGX compute servers, and Cisco's QDD-400G-DR4-S 400G optics with prominent blue and yellow cabling. The host continues, emphasizing, 'This is real, we have built this,' and encourages viewers to 'look at all that beautiful cabling and fiber,' directly referencing the images on the slide.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video begins with a detailed technical slide titled 'Deployment Architecture' from Cisco Live!, showcasing a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)'. The diagram illustrates various network components including Storage Network (100Gb), In-band Network (100Gb), and OSM Network (10Gb), with specific Cisco and SONiC hardware like Cisco 8100 series, Nexus 9k, and ISR 4K, as well as Nvidia Optic modules. The slide includes a legend identifying different hardware and optical modules. The scene then transitions to a live stage presentation where two male hosts, one in a dark suit and the other in a gray suit, are standing on a dark stage with a large audience in the background. The stage lighting is dark with blue and purple hues, and the Cisco Live! logo is visible in the top right corner throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, two presenters, Oliver Tuszik and Jeetu Patel, command a circular white stage under blue and purple lights. A massive curved screen behind them displays a 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster' slide, showcasing Cisco 8000 series, Silicon One devices, AI cluster network fabrics, Nvidia DGX servers, and Cisco's 400G optic. Oliver, a bearded man in a gray suit, gestures expressively, explaining that the AI cluster is "up and running" and they've created a reusable blueprint. Jeetu, a bald man in a dark suit and clear-framed glasses, holding a small black device, responds with animated hand gestures, emphasizing the "real" and functional nature of the setup. He states that Cisco runs "all our AI stuff on this one," enabling every employee to utilize AI tools like Chat GPT and other models. Oliver then details the secure implementation, confirming Chat GPT and co-pilot are available to employees via a private tenant, where all prompts are controlled, logged, and audited, ensuring no data leaves the corporate network or is used for public training, all executed in a "safe and secure way." The camera dynamically alternates between wide shots and close-ups, capturing their energetic discussion, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, dressed in a dark blazer over a light blue collared shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with dynamic blue and red vertical light bars. Wearing a headset microphone, he gestures expressively, explaining how they are leveraging "the power of an LLM, combine it with techniques like retrieval augmented generation and use some of our proprietary information to really add value" for things like product information and case notes. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right. The camera then pulls back to a wide shot of the circular white stage, revealing Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, standing opposite Jeetu. Oliver, also gesturing, asks, "are you using this in your team AI to be more productive, faster, better?" As the two engage in a dynamic conversation, walking slightly toward each other, Jeetu confirms, "Yeah, for sure. We are taking advantage of the productivity benefits of things like AI in the Webex suite, AI Ops and things like Catalyst Center," elaborating on automating tasks such as attenuating radios on wireless access points from "once every couple of months" to "every 30 seconds by itself." The audience is visible as silhouettes under blue lighting in the foreground, while large screens above display the speakers' live feeds, all within the professional and energetic Innovation Theater, enhanced by dynamic blue and purple stage lighting.
