segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with dynamic vertical light bars shifting colors behind him. He gestures expressively while holding a small device, asking in a clear voice, "So, let's jump to the next hot topic, sustainability... did you get an extra budget to deliver on this one?" The Cisco Live! logo is visible. As he speaks, he turns and walks left, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt, enters the frame, smiling. Jeetu takes over the presentation, responding in a measured tone, "Uh, I I did not get an extra budget to deliver this," to which Oliver interjects, "I assume it's still expected that you deliver." Jeetu then elaborates, explaining that beyond being important for the planet, there are practical reasons for the IT department to aggressively pursue sustainability, citing the significant power increase from 5-10 kilowatts per 42U rack for typical data centers to 60-70 kilowatts for high-performance AI GPU clusters. The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the circular white stage in a large, dimly lit auditorium filled with an engaged audience, illuminated by blue spotlights and large screens displaying close-ups of the speakers. Jeetu continues to speak with animated hand gestures, moving slightly, while Oliver stands beside him, holding his device and listening attentively, occasionally nodding. The atmosphere is professional and energetic, reinforced by deep blue tones and colorful vertical light bars consistent with the Cisco Live! branding.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with dynamic vertical light bars shifting in color behind him. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a circular stage at Cisco Live!, bathed in dramatic dark blue lighting and accented by vertical neon light bars in red, green, and blue. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, stands on the right, gesturing animatedly while holding a small device. He speaks with a confident voice, explaining how to get "as much power as possible everywhere else from the top of rack down," confirming that "it is something where we're..." Jeetu Patel, bald and in a dark suit, listens attentively on the left, his hands clasped. Jeetu then interjects with an inquisitive tone, stating, "But you need so you need to do it anyhow because otherwise you would not be able to manage energy cost and energy capacity." Oliver quickly agrees with "Exactly." The camera alternates between close-ups of Oliver speaking passionately and wider shots showing both men. Jeetu then playfully challenges Oliver, asking if he's "customer zero" and "not allowed to buy anything else than Cisco, correct?" Oliver, with a smile, confidently replies, "Well, I why would I need to buy anything else? It doesn't even occur to me to buy anything else." Jeetu then poses a more serious question to Oliver, asking for his advice to "all the CIOs, the IT leader in the room" on how to "get the benefit out of the entire Cisco portfolio." Oliver begins to elaborate, referencing "a complex end-to-end problem like hybrid work" and listing various related challenges such as "remote access, survivable connectivity, device management, productivity, cyber security, SD-WAN." Throughout, the Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner, and the audience is seen seated in rows around the stage, engaged in the professional and immersive presentation environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark suit over a black shirt, speaking animatedly on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He gestures with his hands clasped, then turns to his right, gesturing toward a large screen displaying abstract green and blue visuals. Jeetu's articulate voice discusses the complexity of fragmented solutions, stating, "You heard Jonathan talk about the value of the platform and G2 talk about how fragmented some of these spaces are... That's a lot of complexity... if I don't have to be the integrator... and I can just take advantage of the entire integrated platform, that's saving me complexity and making the IT job a lot simpler." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the expansive auditorium, filled with a large, attentive audience seated in rows under dim blue lighting. The stage is illuminated by spotlights, and a massive curved screen behind the stage displays Jeetu’s image and presentation content. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, joins him on stage, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively while speaking, engaging with Jeetu. Oliver, with a distinct voice and a slight accent, adds, "Perfect, I think there's nothing more to add on such a summary. Driving the complexity down with a portfolio that goes end-to-end." He then thanks the audience, "Thanks to all of you for joining us. The show will continue and I'm looking forward to seeing you all tomorrow. Have a great day." The camera alternates between close-ups of Oliver and wider shots showing both men interacting. The curved screen then displays a slide for a 'Guest Keynote' featuring Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, with the title 'AI, Big Data, & Humanity: Delivering on the Promise' and the time 'Tomorrow 17:15'. The audience responds with enthusiastic applause and cheering, followed by upbeat, energetic background music, as the professional and dynamic atmosphere, underscored by the Cisco Live! branding, continues.

Oliver Tuszik describes it as 'one of the best, if not the best soccer stadium ever,' 'fully digitized,' focused on the 'best fan experience,' and 'supported by the full Cisco stack'.

entity_library.Curved Video Screen Display: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. In this segment, it first mirrors the detailed transcript interface, showing a marketing meeting discussion, then transitions to a vibrant title slide 'Reimagining the IT experience with AI', and finally displays the 'Bringing everything together for IT' slide, visually supporting Jeetu Patel's presentation on AI's pervasive role and its benefits for IT.
entity_library.Jonathan Davidson: A man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt and gray trousers, speaking on stage with expressive hand gestures; identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, whose voice is heard passionately discussing the strategy for Cisco Networking Cloud and Cisco Security Cloud, and introducing Jeetu Patel.
entity_library.Presentation Slide: A slide displayed on the curved screen featuring the text 'Turning challenges into opportunities' over an image of green sprouts, followed by slides on 'Data centers' and 'Smart buildings'. Adele Trombetta verbally references the specific metrics and goals presented on these slides, such as 'Reduce 40% power capacity' for data centers and 'Reduce 50% power consumption' for smart buildings, directly aligning her speech with the visual content.
entity_library.Global Montage: A sequence of symbolic visuals including a nighttime port with cranes and shipping containers, a close-up of a green sprout emerging from soil, and a crowded city street, used to illustrate themes of global connectivity, growth, and urban life.
entity_library.Formula 1 Pit Crew: A team of mechanics in bright orange uniforms and blue helmets, performing a rapid tire change on a racing car during a pit stop, with 'DP WORLD' and 'Cisco' branding visible on the car and backdrop. This dynamic footage is shown on the stage screens, visually illustrating Jonathan Davidson's analogy of driving a 'Formula 1 race car'.
entity_library.Experience Insights Dashboard: A detailed interface titled 'Experience Insights Powered by ThousandEyes,' displaying real-time data on endpoints including user names, locations, health status, latency, jitter, loss, CPU, and memory usage, with some endpoints marked as 'Healthy' and others as 'Offline.' Jonathan Davidson explains its purpose is to show "performance data" and help users "understand how you can look at any device and how that device impacts the applications and vice versa," which is "key for rapid issue resolution."
entity_library.Configuration Interface: A user-friendly interface titled 'Monitor Critical Applications for SD-WAN,' showing steps to connect ThousandEyes, select applications like Salesforce, and configure monitoring, with a green header and clear navigation, where 'active application monitoring' is selected and 'ThousandEyes is activated on MX devices' for network visibility.
entity_library.Path Visualization Graph: A detailed network trace diagram from ThousandEyes, showing multiple agents (e.g., MX-Branch-1, MX-Branch-2) connecting to a target server (https://abc.salesforce.com/), with nodes color-coded by status (red for errors, green for success) and lines indicating data paths, including a highlighted node 'xe-3-3-2-dcr1-lon.net.sfdc.net' with metrics like 84% forwarding loss and 73.3 ms average response; it is presented as a tool that provides 'this level of detail' to 'highlight wireless networks, identify real-time issues across multiple domains, and go deep into third-party SaaS application performance.'
entity_library.Meraki Dashboard Interface: A user interface showing network monitoring data including wireless usage, client connections, and a world map, with a green header bar and detailed metrics, explicitly named "the Meraki dashboard" by the presenter, who highlights its utility for faster analysis and fixes.
entity_library.Packet Capture Interface: A detailed interface for initiating a packet capture on a specific switch, with fields for switch selection, ports, duration, filter expressions, and a 'Start capture' button, along with sample filter expressions and a note on maximum capture duration, which Jonathan Davidson highlights as a key troubleshooting feature within the Meraki Dashboard.
entity_library.L'Oréal: A brand prominently featured with a dark blue slide stating 'The number one beauty company in the world,' accompanied by a close-up of a woman’s face and a hand applying product. The presenter identifies it as a long-standing Cisco customer "embracing this platform approach and the Cisco networking cloud," and Charles Croft later describes it as "the largest beauty company in the world today" aiming to be "the number one beauty tech company," with "its own research departments, factories, retail, headquarters."
entity_library.Charles Croft: A man with short hair, wearing a dark blazer over a floral shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting with red and white chairs in the background, identified as Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal. He speaks about the simplicity and cost-effectiveness of Meraki systems, stating that the TCO is about half compared to legacy systems, allowing them to 'do more with less'.
entity_library.L'Oréal Manufacturing Line: An automated production line with robotic arms filling and capping bottles, showcasing the company’s industrial operations with a focus on precision and efficiency.
entity_library.L'Oréal Mobile App Interface: A digital interface for hair analysis, displaying a circular scan area, user objectives, and a 'Learn More' section, emphasizing personalized beauty solutions.
entity_library.Product Application Scene: A close-up of a man applying a product to a woman’s hair, with the word 'SCAN' overlaid in large white letters, highlighting the brand’s focus on technology and personalization.
entity_library.Cisco Networking Hardware: Close-up shots of Cisco switches with multiple ports and a wall-mounted white Cisco access point, emphasizing the physical infrastructure supporting the network solutions.
entity_library.Smartphone Interface: A hand holding a smartphone displaying a 'Wall screen' app with real-time power metrics such as voltage, current, and energy usage, visually demonstrating the capability Charles Croft describes: 'I can turn off any device anywhere in Europe... from my mobile phone. That's the power of having the dashboard and having the app.'
entity_library.Retail Environment: A modern retail setting with a woman in a pink striped shirt interacting with a digital display and a tablet at a checkout counter, illustrating the seamless customer experience enabled by the 'connectivity platform' that Charles Croft describes as including 'sensors and CCTV.'
entity_library.Urban Street Scene: A London street with a red double-decker bus and modern buildings, including the Gherkin, followed by a view from inside the bus, visually accompanying Charles Croft's statement about managing devices 'from the top of a bus going through London.'
entity_library.Desk Workstation: A person working at a desk with a dual-monitor setup displaying network management software, including a 'Migrated C9300-LP' interface, visually representing the 'automation' and 'API' capabilities Charles Croft discusses for simplifying network management.
entity_library.Deployment Graphic: A blue graphic with white text stating '3500 devices in 3 days,' surrounded by icons of connected devices, perfectly synchronizing with Charles Croft's enthusiastic declaration, 'Patching three and a half thousand devices in three days is fantastic.'
entity_library.Cisco Secure Access Credential Dialog: A pop-up window titled 'Cisco Secure Access Credential' with fields for 'API Key' and 'Secret', visually shown as Jonathan Davidson describes connecting branches and applying security policies.
entity_library.Policies Configuration Screen: A software interface displaying the 'Policies' page with a 'US-West Branches' policy group, showing options for 'Application Priority & SLA', 'Embedded Security', and 'Routing', with a 'Deploy' button and a 'Success' notification confirming deployment to 20 sites, all demonstrated visually as Jonathan Davidson explains the deployment process.
entity_library.Security Dashboard Interface: A multi-panel screen displaying live surveillance footage from various locations, including train platforms and crowded areas, which appears on the screen as Jonathan Davidson discusses the use of Meraki cameras by a European rail operator for physical security.
entity_library.Meraki Vision Interface: A detailed user interface for Cisco Meraki Vision, initially showing a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station with filters for time, cameras, people (top and bottom clothing colors), and vehicles (type and color). It is demonstrated through Jonathan Davidson's explanation of its ability to filter on objects, use color attributes (e.g., 'find me the person in the red shirt'), and detect safety incidents, such as a person in an orange jacket on train tracks, by recognizing unauthorized entry into prohibited areas without employee uniforms. The interface also features sliders for 'Object 1' match precision and 'Minimum event duration', and is shown in both grid and 'List View' modes.
entity_library.Announcement Slide: A dark blue slide with a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and white text stating 'New innovations in AI' with bullet points: 'Securing AI: intellectual property protection,' 'Advanced AI email threat detection,' and 'AI Assistant in Cisco Secure Access,' set against abstract blue and yellow shapes, which Jeetu references directly when discussing the AI Assistant's capabilities.
